> have a read of this: [https://www.bicycling.com/training/a20043122/why-i-love-to-zwift/](https://www.bicycling.com/training/a20043122/why-i-love-to-zwift/)
>
> I've been reading the latest book by Yuval Noah Harari - "21 lessons for the 21st Century". He makes a very good point about modern times - "we are upgrading our computers but downgrading humans".
>
> Likewise, I think we are upgrading our training equipment but downgrading the athlete.
>
> If you don't get sunlight, don't get cold, don't feel the earth, don't hear the wind or feel the rain..... you are losing control of your circadian rhythm, raising inflammation, deactivating neurotransmitters and not expressing certain genes. That's just some of the things.
>
> I have stated this point from the very beginning, its on my "about" page.
>
> "I want to help make you a better athlete, the current model is flawed."
>
> Continued........on The Connected Athlete [https://www.patreon.com/connectedathlete/posts](https://www.patreon.com/connectedathlete/posts)

Great points and well stated.
I always feel better outside, feeling the elements and nature.
The other day driving home I had to just pull over and run, play, jump, climb down some
Nature trails. All of 30 minutes and I felt a world of difference. A quick recharge!

Keep it coming Barry.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-05-11T11:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/post-270182>

Boil it all down to fewest possible words. This is a typical days routine that can be adapted to your own personal situations.

The body likes routine. Getting used to the same times. Having got to sleep around 10pm, you can wake fully refreshed and rested around sunrise.

Head straight outside as soon as wake up wearing as little as possible, get down on the ground skin touching the earth start doing 100 burpees. Build up a sweat and lay on your back looking up at the sunrise breathing correctly.

Next have a cold water shower outside in the sun. Dry off have a good bowel movement about 8am then get dressed and go to work or whatever you do. Loop all your metabolic water all day. Try to be outside as much as possible look at the sun as much as you can through the day. Fast only looping orin all day until 5pm consume your OMAD until 6pm.

Watch the sunset, and from then on wear blue blocking red glasses until 10 when you go to sleep inside a silver faraday canopy.

### kris90 — 2020-01-02T19:29:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/post-284152>

> [ssj3 said:](/goto/post?id=129392)
>
> This reads very similar to my experience.
>
> I had survived for years on a keto diet (eventually a story for my optimal journal to put in context) but not when supporting high volume/intensity/density weight training. But when I switched to a keto diet again, I didn’t adjust for many factors (loss of calories specifically one - I actually wanted to test the calories don’t matter spin) and training really suffered. Also, my labs tanked, couldn’t sustain let alone add muscle, body comp worsened and I was starting to have to take days off due to poor recovery.
>
> Now about 8 months in, consuming a lot more fat, a few more carbs (about 50-70 grams a day) and about 800-1000 calories per day more, things are starting to turn good. Leaner with weight up about 4kg. Now I don’t know if this is just the calories as I did start doing other things like eating oysters or muscles nearly daily, infrared therapy, 8-10 hours of sleep, etc.
>
> Still some bio-hacking to do as recovery and ability to maintain intensity still needs to improve, but I like where this is going.

This has been my experience also. I have found CT/keto adapted works wonders for staying slim as if hibernating and maximizing autophagy. Keeps hunger levels down so fasting becomes a breeze. However, while it's helped keep me lean, when it comes to lifting heavy (strength training), I'd tend to plateau. Since then I've significantly increasing my caloric intake, and upped protein and the gains have been ridiculous. I still keep carbs low (50g or under on training days). I've gained about 15 lbs in just under 3 months, and have made some incredible strength gains. My moods have improved and I definitely feel like my T levels have surged, although I have not measured. I have to forcefeed myself most of the time, but I am staying lean and putting on mass. These are gains I normally wouldn't have made naturally (I have a history of AAS use in my bodybuilding days) and I've been accused of juicing again.

The only thing I have to ensure is that I rest long enough between sets to replenish ATP. That's usually 3 mins minimal, and potentially up to 6-8 mins between heavy sets of squats. I'm also modifying my volume as my intensity creeps up. I may experiment with some nicotine pre-workout and see how that works out for me.

If the time crystals that control the nuclear genome are yoked to the solar cycles, cardio can be beneficial. Are you hungry when you wake in the morning? If you can’t eat till later your clocks might be off. Short reps of heavy weights can help slow these crystal clocks down. Cardio not so much. When it comes to the sun timing is everything. AM sun is nature’s metronome. This is where all the hormones are regulated by the Leptin receptor in the SCN. Then getting that initial UVA signal at around 9AM turns off the hormone production. If you start up the engine at 7AM but don’t turn it off at 9 you run out of gas. Maybe Cortisol is stealing your testosterone.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-09-11T18:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasted-state-training-adaptations.297/post-326243>

**TODAY'S LESSON:** POMC arose over 500 million years ago by the insertion of the melanocortin sequences into a prepro-endorphin gene. Evidence for this comes from structural identities with other opioid precursors in both the NH2- and COOH-terminal regions of POMC.
The phenomena of pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC) as a hormone precursor emerged gradually over time as observations slowly filled in pieces of the puzzle. Long before the concept of hormone precursors was realized, the bronzed skin color described by Addison in his patient with adrenal insufficiency (“melasma suprarenale”) gave perhaps the first hints of a connection between the hypothalamic, pituitary, adrenal (HPA) axis and skin color. A similar link between the pituitary and pigmentation came from the studies of Allen in 1916 and Smith in 1916 who both noted that immersing tadpoles in pituitary extract made their skins darker. In humans, too, large doses of the porcine pituitary extract also appeared to cause pigmentation in 1954, with this active extract of the pars intermedia of the pituitary henceforth termed “melanocyte-stimulating hormone” or MSH.
In 1932, Cushing extended his clinical reports of a polyglandular syndrome caused by basophilic adenomas of the pituitary by linking this finding with adrenal hyperactivity. In the 1930s, work by Ingle and Kendall in the 1930s showed that administration of large amounts of “cortin,” a purified adrenal extract, produced atrophy of the adrenal cortex in rats. Importantly, they found that administration of the “adrenotropic principle” of the anterior pituitary was effective in preventing adrenal cortical regression following treatment with cortin. The first hints of a behavioral angle to POMC biology came from studies by Ferrari in the 1950s, when “stretching-yawning syndrome,” a bizarre crisis of muscular tone, occurred following the central administration of MSH. Many other studies assessing the effects of central α-MSH on motivational processes followed, but it was not until 1976 that Panskepp observed for the first time that this peptide decreased food intake using light alone. What else might POMC do in a modern world run by artificial light now? Today, you get to see another perspective centralized science missed.

This highlights why blaming diet is a losing proposition when it is light behind the effect. This mistake is made by many on this forum even the ones who are members.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-19T21:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-2916>

In the efforts to eat more water food, is everything good - or do we need to focus on cold water varieties? Also, any hints for canned/tinned seafood consumption?

### hazyjane — 2012-03-19T22:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-2932>

I've been eating a lot more cold water fatty fish like canned Alaskan pink salmon (I like to make salmon cakes with it,), trout and smoked sardines in olive oil. I've been eating more shrimp, too, for the iodine.

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T01:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-3001>

I'm going mostly for the salt-water fishy's. They have more iodine and omega 3. Fresh water fish is probably fine, too, but mostly what you get in the grocery store is Talapia and that just seems like a crap fish to me, but I may be wrong. This summer I will be eating lots of fresh caught trout and pike.

### Adriana — 2012-03-24T09:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-5343>

> **Cavemam;2926 wrote:** Also, any hints for canned/tinned seafood consumption?

I made some great salmon patties this week with canned salmon:

1 large can salmon

2 eggs

2 tbsp coconut flour

1 tsp dijon mustard

3 sliced green onions

Brown in a skillet in butter. Nom!

### Adriana — 2012-03-24T09:25:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-seafood-or-just-cold-waters-seafood.298/post-5345>

> **Cavemam;2926 wrote:** In the efforts to eat more water food, is everything good - or do we need to focus on cold water varieties? Also, any hints for canned/tinned seafood consumption?

I made some great salmon patties this week with canned salmon?

1 large can salmon

2 eggs

2 tbsp coconut flour

1 tbsp lemon juice

1 tsp dijon mustard

3 sliced green onions

Brown in a skillet in butter. Nom!

and Gee-ann, I've been to Merrillville!! a couple of years ago we were going to NW Indiana for business, had a house in Hobart! GROOVY!

How much did you lose Owl?? I've got about 80lbs to go, hoping that it goes fast!!! (sillly I know, it's not like I became fat overnight, but I'm soooo completely over all this and ready for it to be over!)

I'm about 22 days in (started March 1st) and down 9lbs, I'm sleeping better, cravings are gone as long as I eat my BAB,

happy happy joy joy

what does your post reset diet/meals look like???

# Thread: Question re: LR and IF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### esam5 — 2012-03-19T22:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-2941>

I am 3 weeks into the reset. I was reading over at another forum where Dr. K is also active where IF is not reccomended until leptin sensitivity is reset as it can cause harm. here is my situation, then my question is at the end......

my meals are 7am, 1pm and 7pm ( I usually go to bed around 10:30 or so....)

I eat a BAB, about 50gms protein, low carbs, and generous fat for satiety ( want to increase my protein to lose weight)

I do not snack, it has on occassion been a challenge, but I truly have been faithful.

my other meals are generally very low carb as carbs trigger cravings. I eat predominately meat, eggs, more meat!! I do have butter, mayo, CO, and some cream in my morning coffee. I keep protein shakes to a minimum, and I'm working to eliminate cheese. ( as it isn't paleo)

I'm trying to keep my water intake up and I try not to have anything processed ( triggers)

my goal is to lose about 80lbs ...I'm 5'9" and am 248.

Sometimes, after lunch, my stomach is upset and at dinner time I'm not hungry, so I won't eat. last night was a good example, I didn't feel true hunger until around 10pm then it was too late, so I went to bed without eating. Lunch was around 2pm. I'm not really fasting on purpose, but should I have eaten something small around 7pm? or is it ok to skip if I wasn't hungry? I'd say in the last 3 weeks, I've only skipped a meal three times. it is usually dinner, or lunch. Never breakfast.

I'm really committed to this program, I'm very strict with my eating schedule and with 5 kids in the house, I'm doing really well to giving up snacking!!!!!

### Owl — 2012-03-20T07:18:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3161>

Intermittent fasting. Skipping meals.

### Owl — 2012-03-20T07:27:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3163>

> **esam5;2951 wrote:** Sometimes, after lunch, my stomach is upset and at dinner time I'm not hungry, so I won't eat. last night was a good example, I didn't feel true hunger until around 10pm then it was too late, so I went to bed without eating. Lunch was around 2pm. I'm not really fasting on purpose, but should I have eaten something small around 7pm? or is it ok to skip if I wasn't hungry? I'd say in the last 3 weeks, I've only skipped a meal three times. it is usually dinner, or lunch. Never breakfast.

The question is, why is your stomach upset after lunch? Are you eating too large of a meal?

It's ok to eat just breakfast and lunch. Or just breakfast and dinner. The goal is to eventually eat two meals per day, and breakfast and late lunch is fine. Some people go to two meals right away, some take longer.

Dr. K doesn't want you to IF if you're hungry. But if you're not, go ahead and skip a meal.

If you get into the habit of being hungry late at night, go ahead and eat a small snack for dinner. You don't want to go to bed hungry every night.

I usually eat only breakfast and a late lunch. But if I feel like I didn't eat enough for lunch, I'll have just a few bites of meat for dinner to keep from getting hungry later.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-20T15:34:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3217>

It's OK to skip dinner if you are not hungry. And it's OK to eliminate snacking for the children too. Snacking sets up lifelong behaviors and takes the emphasis off eating a good meal.

### esam5 — 2012-03-20T20:14:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3392>

> **Owl;3185 wrote:** The question is, why is your stomach upset after lunch? Are you eating too large of a meal?

that may be it, but I had my gall bladder removed last year and sometimes I get those acid-y burps and such. I get heartburn still and sometimes get nauseaus. Even right before I get "hungry" I feel a bit nauseus. I don't know what exactly is going on. it goes away, but is uncomfortable.

I will try to cut back on portion size, and I am cutting out dairy as well,. I really want to get to just eating 2 meals a day, it would make me happy.

my hunger will go away, it doesn't keep gnawing at me.... I do notice when I don't have enough protein at breakfast I get more hungry during the day. thats why I like my BAB to set the pace......

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T20:28:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3402>

> **esam5;3416 wrote:** that may be it, but I had my gall bladder removed last year and sometimes I get those acid-y burps and such. I get heartburn still and sometimes get nauseaus. Even right before I get "hungry" I feel a bit nauseus. I don't know what exactly is going on. it goes away, but is uncomfortable.
>
> I will try to cut back on portion size, and I am cutting out dairy as well,. I really want to get to just eating 2 meals a day, it would make me happy.
>
>
>
> my hunger will go away, it doesn't keep gnawing at me.... I do notice when I don't have enough protein at breakfast I get more hungry during the day. thats why I like my BAB to set the pace......

For what it's worth, I had my gallbladder out in November of 2008 and it took the best part of two years for my system to find its "new normal" in terms of digestion. Doctors seem to like to tell people "Oh, you don't need a gallbladder", but that's not _actually_ true. It has a very specific function and your body DOES need it. When you have it taken out, your body has to adapt to NOT having it, which can take quite a while, depending on a lot of factors.

### Owl — 2012-03-20T20:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3427>

I'm not an expert on this, but have you tried Betaine HCL? I know a lot of people here find heartburn/GERD much better when using HCL. Reflux is usually caused by too little stomach acid.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T04:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-3760>

or if you don\`t have a gallbladder I believe purified oxbile is recommended as well

### esam5 — 2012-03-22T18:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-re-lr-and-if.300/post-4507>

thanks for the suggestions,

I have not tried either of those. I will take a prilosec once in awhile. I have found that I tend to get reflux several hours after I eat. and I may be having a bit too big of a meal, but certainly no larger than I would have eaten prior to this WOE.

yesterdays late lunch was about a 7-8oz steak, grilled, and 3 small romaine leaves chopped, and a 2inch piece of cucumber cut up, some italian dressing and SF jello cup. Considering that I had only 2 meals yesterday, that seemed fine for me, but about 3-4 hours after I ate I was burping up my salad. (yeah, I know, GROSS) sorry.

good to know that it will take awhile to get to my new normal.

I was very surprised that they removed my gall bladder, I had only the one attack, but it was a doosey, was in the ER, then admitted me and my test results even 24hours later were pretty high, it was very inflamed. (ice cream was the last thing I ate before the attack, I have no desire to eat it ever again!!!)

I assume these are both supplements I can get at my local healthfood store???

### SimonM — 2012-03-19T23:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-we-need-to-work-our-local-groups.302/post-2958>

> Most of us arenÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t lucky enough to have a community of Paleo friends living in our neighborhood. Some of us are the only Paleo people we know in real life. This is why events like PaleoFX are so important. Sure, weÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re learning. Sure, weÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re meeting people that have changed our lives. Sure, we might learn a new recipe or two, but itÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s really meeting people that are just like us that is the true value of an event like this for me. We know that our ancestors lived in communities, were raised by the village and had support from all sides. We donÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t live in that world anymore. WeÃ¢â‚¬â„¢re alone in our boxes, separated from family by oceans and even further isolated from society because we wear weird shoes and donÃ¢â‚¬â„¢t eat the same food as everyone else. I think joining a CrossFit box is akin to joining a Church for some of us: itÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s the only community we have.

[http://www.primalgirl.com/2012/03/19/paleo-fx-2012-five-people-who-will-change-your-life/](http://http://www.primalgirl.com/2012/03/19/paleo-fx-2012-five-people-who-will-change-your-life/)

# Thread: Frozen shrimp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### L8F — 2012-03-19T23:52:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-2960>

So I eat a lot of frozen wild shrimp. It is easy and tasty. BUT, I cannot seem to get it to dry out before sautÃƒÂ©ing. Then it turns more like a watery cooked shrimp. Still tasty, but not the oily, buttery sautÃƒÂ©ed shrimp that I am looking for. Any suggestions? Baking in oven to dry, will that work? And how long?

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T02:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3029>

It's kind of a pain in the butt, but if you thaw them out in cold running water and then let them sit in the fridge (loosely covered) for a few hours before you want to cook with them, it should help.

Or, take some paper towel or a clean tea towel and pat the shrimp dry once they're thawed, right before you toss them in the pan. This is what I usually do.

### L8F — 2012-03-20T19:20:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3354>

Thanks! I was thinking about it this AM while still sleepy, and wondered if steaming them cooked, and THEN pan frying would solve the excess water leak issue...Also pain in the butt, but might rid the excess water. I am dreaming of a scampi type thing,

### ealachan — 2012-03-20T19:30:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3368>

You'd probably overcook them if you did it that way. Overcooking turns them really chewy. Shrimp, like most seafood, is not very forgiving when it comes to overcooking. There's a pretty small margin between "cooked just enough" and "ooh, shrimp-shaped erasers in garlic sauce!"

When I'm cooking seafood, I tend to err on the side of very slight undercooking, because the carry-over heat from the pan and the plate will finish the cooking by the time you go to actually eat the dish.

### Souldanzer — 2012-03-21T01:05:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3612>

Don't waste that yummy broth that comes out of frozen shrimp!

I use frozen, too. I never really sauté them for exactly that reason but throw the whole bag frozen into a pot, boil them (they will release enough fluid), then drain the broth, put it in the blender with some spices and fat, whizz and use it as a sauce/soup.

You could probably throw them in to a frying pan after this process but I think there really is the danger of overcooking if you're sensitive to that.

### L8F — 2012-03-21T03:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3713>

> **ealachan;3392 wrote:** There's a pretty small margin between "cooked just enough" and "ooh, shrimp-shaped erasers in garlic sauce!"

Hahahaha. Thanks guys, I guess I will just suck it up and go with saucy slightly watery shimp sautés. Will have to find a good wild source restaurant for scampi once I decide to retry butter.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-21T03:28:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-3726>

Salt! You thaw them, salt them, then leave them on a napkin/towel in the fridge. Alternately, you brine them then just paper towel dry them.

### L8F — 2012-03-22T14:38:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-4323>

Cool!! Thanks. Will try tonight. Have some defrosting in fridge

### Groketteliveson — 2012-03-22T15:32:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-4352>

When you can have butter again I've got a really good Cajun shrimp recipe to share. \*It supposed to be shrimp and grits but I don't make the grits portion of it. \*In the past I substituted rice for the grits. \*I was thinking you could probably substitute shredded cauliflour if you wanted instead. \*But since it's cooked in a sauce it wouldnt matter if you shrimp turned out watery or not.

Peel and devain about a pound of shrimp or more

Coat shrimp with about 2 Tblsp. Of Tonys Louisianna Cajun seasoning. \*I use this brand because the other brands can be too salty! \*You will also \*have to play around with the seasoning according to your own tastes.

Also throw in for the coating

1Tbsp. Italian seasoning

1Tbsp of paprika and some pepper to to taste

Saute the shrimp in a pan with some melted butter and minced garlic till cooked. \*Pull out and set aside.

With all the drippings still in the pan you can make a sauce if you wish by adding a little bit of cornstarch( or some other appropriate thickener) and cook for about 5 to 10 minutes till a light color brown. \*Slowly add some chicken stock and some heavy whipping cream, add a little bit of worcestershire \*sauce and maybe some hot sauce to taste. \*You can add bacon or ham to the sauce if you wish.

When sauce is finished mix the shrimp in the sauce or just drizzle the sauce over the shrimp and over the bed of cauliflower.

I have to say it is really spicy so if you like spice it's great! \*

### L8F — 2012-03-22T16:12:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-4382>

yum....thi is the stuff I have been craving. I am making do with shrimp in ghee and chopped garlic and turmeric. Gotta get cooking.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-29T16:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frozen-shrimp.303/post-8258>

I stocked up on shrimp last week when Whole Foods had their one-day special in them. I've been steaming them until almost done and then sauteeing. Seems to work well. They're not soggy anyway!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3053>

I'll report in as a guinea pig

I've been on hcg for 9 days now and have lost about 6.5#'s. Wierd thing this time was I lost during loading so now I'm not showing the huge first week losses as the usual. I think my body was so used to the lots of fats that it didn't blink when I ate all those avocados and steaks on load days. LOL

Doing the timing of Leptin Rx by eating one pc of protein within 30 minutes of waking. Nothing in between meals and 4 - 5 hrs in between. I have my veg at noon and then for dinner my protein and veggie. No fruit, since I do no sugar anyway as a rule while eating paleo and no glutin so no melba. So far so good. I have been trying to get more serious about my Cold Therapy, but like most that are hypothryoid and doing hcg, I'm not liking the cold so much. I know once I get cold adapted in a few weeks the weight will hopefully really move. I have about 25 to 30#'s I would love to lose for good and hopefully in this one 45 day round! This is my fourth go round with hcg in the last 2 yrs. I've lost about 25 to 30 pounds each time and have gained it back again.

Will be interesting to see how everyone stabilizes with doing CT and LRx with Hcg.

\[h=2\]\[/h\]

### SuzieQ — 2012-03-20T03:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3056>

I am doing HCG and CT... I am one week in.

I am following protocol but have omitted fruit and the bread sticks.

I am increasing my proteins to make up for the lessor calories. Keeping it still around 500 a day.

I am eating just 2 meals a day. Breakfast and dinner (between 3-5pm)

I am drinking green & yerbamate tea throughout the day and one cold-dripped coffee a day.

I am doing spot CT'ing around my complete middle section 1-2x a day and 30 minutes a night in the pool up to my waist.

I have been doing CT since early Feb. and I do find it harder to "want" to do it during HCG because I am usually more chilly on this protocol. I have done HCG 3 x before. Struggled with stabilizing. I only have 10 to lose but I wanted to see if CT would help with the stabilizing part. I have hormone and thryoid issues in the midst of all this.

If you have any further suggestions as to how to make this the perfect successful round... I am all ears! Teach me!

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T03:07:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3062>

> **August;3064 wrote: Oh, while I was looking for the passage re: feeling cold . . I found this, which I thought was interesting: (under the
>
>
> > Dr Simeons said:
> >
> > **The Exhaustion of the Fat-bank**
> >
> > But there is still a third way in which obesity can become established, and that is when a presumably normal fat-center is suddenly-the emphasis is on suddenly-called upon to deal with an enormous influx of food far in excess of momentary requirements. At first glance it does seem that here we have a straight-forward case of overeating being responsible for obesity, but on further analysis it soon becomes clear that the relation of cause and effect is not so simple. In the first place we are merely assuming that the capacity of the fat center is normal while it is possible and even probable that the only persons who have some inherited trait in this direction can become obese merely by overeating.
> >
> > Secondly, in many of these cases the amount of loud eaten remains the same and it is only the consumption of fuel which is suddenly decreased, as when an athlete is confined to bed for many weeks with a broken bone or when a man leading a highly active life is suddenly tied to his desk in an office and to television at home. Similarly, when a person, grown up in a cold climate, is transferred to a tropical country and continues to eat as before, he may develop obesity because in the heat far less fuel is required to maintain the normal body temperature.
>
> **
>
> > Dr Simeons said:
> >
> > Source: Pounds and Inches
>
>
>
> Whoa August, very interesting find. I never read this before and will go check out my copy of P & I. Thanks!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-20T05:35:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3134>

I am on my 4th round of hcg, took a long break between 3 & 4 to do the Leptin Rx. I had hoped I could lose on Leptin Rx but that was not the case, but I was stable as a rock and didn't gain more than 2 lbs. When I read the first CT blog I decided to amp up my cold shower, which I'd been doing off and on for about a year, for totally different reasons! I started hcg mid Feb, and lost 10 lbs before doing a 3 day interruption, I am back on now for the last week and then I will move to a Leptin Rx style P3. I am eating 650-750 calories per day. My typical rate of loss averaged out to be .5 per day, and I am still at about that level.

The first week I followed protocol pretty strictly, the second week I started adding cream in my coffee and cut all fruit... losses did slow down about them. For this last week I am cutting the cream and adding back in some limited fruit. I have not experienced one day of crazy hunger like I did in previous rounds... I always had at least one day that was challenging. I'm cold whether I do CT or not, so i figure I might as well do the CT because I feel really good afterwards, and notice when I am warming. I get really warm in the middle of the night and kick the covers off.

### Pamela — 2012-03-20T06:48:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3155>

I've done a few rounds of HCG and am now just finishing my 3rd round. The first round, last year at this time, I lost 20 lbs and within the first 3 days of being on Stage 3 I had a gallbladder attack that took me to the hospital. At that time I knew nothing of Paleo, Primal etc. so had the GB removed and while it could have been salvaged I still am glad I had it out. After several attacks I couldn't take it any more. Then I did another round of HCG last summer and lost another 16 lbs. In Sept of last year is when I found Leptin reset so I tried it but gained weight. Was not happy. Has a physical and found thyroid antibodies high so am now on Armour but only .5 grain.

Now, I am at the very end of the 3rd round and at 160 lbs, 5"9 female. Would still like to lose another 10 lbs but I don't think it will be on HCG. I'm hoping that with the thyroid it will help stabilize my weight. I was waiting to finish the round before doing CT since Dr. K said to have a high fat meal while doing it. So this next week I will start even though I hate cold. Good thing is it is no longer winter here in Chicago and I can deal with the idea of cold water better.

I'm a little leary of jumping to the reset right off of HCG. Colleen would you recommend this?

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T19:34:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3375>

Colleen and Jack

I've never done HCG, but want to give it a shot. I'm 10 yrs post gastric bypass surgery, about 70# to lose. I've been paleo for 9 months. I'm doing ct and reset now. Would you let me know if there is something I should pay attention to, or if there is a reason this would work differently because of surgery? (vagus nerve severed). Thanks!!

### colleencoble — 2012-03-20T19:57:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3388>

We've had tons of people on the forum lose successfully after surgery. My cousin also. The main problem you will have is after the round. Many surgery patients have trouble getting down enough meat after the surgery. They fill up too fast to get down the meat and fats they need. So that may be a challenge for you.

Hi, I am new here and have been reading this forum for a few days now with great interest. I did a round of HCG a year ago and lost 38 lbs and about 28 inches all over, dropped 4 sizes in clothes and thought this was the best thing ever. I kept the weight off over a three week vacation with no trouble and through the next month. After that I noticed the weight and mostly the inches came back on gradually but over the next four months I gained all but five pounds back! Grrr, not a happy camper. On Feb. 25, 2012 I decided to do another round of HCG while doing CT (ice packs on stomach for that nasty belly fat). Two weeks in I lost 12 lbs but not without feeling starved the entire time and angry that I couldn't eat and felt horrible. On March 10th (the last day of being 49 yo) I gave up HCG and started the Leptin Rx instead. Loved that I could eat again and felt wonderful. I'm now 10 days in and haven't lost any inches, my weight has gone up one, down one, up one, down one, etc. for the last 10 days. I'm considering going back on HCG and doing CT but increasing my protein and very little veggies. Is this going to screw up my brain and any progress I am making that I may not see yet? Sorry this was kind of long, thank you for any advice I can get!

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T00:13:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3570>

I don't need brand name.  That's the typical dose but it often needs lowered with each round. Try skipping your hcg tomorrow and see how you feel by evening.

Paleowanna, if that's mixed the way doctors often do, that's 200 iu and too high for most people so that's why you were hungry.  If you do it again, we'll talk about dosing. I would start you on 150 and you might need to go down from there.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T00:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3587>

Paleowanna, that's a mistake most doctors make. They don't seem to have read the part in Pounds and Inches about too much causing the reverse effect. We see it all the time on the big forum. What exactly are you eating now?

Suzie, by afternoon and into the evening, you should be feeling no hunger. If that's the case take the dose to .4. If you have hunger and it continues into the evening, then the dose is too low and you go up to .6.

Good luck!

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T05:52:06+0200

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T06:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3793>

it's a common misconception that you are supposed to do high fat loading days. That's not really what the protocol states. He lists lots of things, not just fatty foods. Pastries, chocolate, and bread for example. Which of course we will never do.  Well maybe the chocolate!

Pounds and Inches says this: Small stones in the gall bladder may in patients who have recently had typical colics cause more frequent colics under treatment with HCG. This may be due to the almost complete absence of fat from the diet, which prevents the normal emptying of the gall bladder. '

So basically, it can cause more problems when there is an existing problem.

### JoanneA — 2012-03-21T16:26:59+0200

Hey Colleen, i mention Robins book because it is a secind hypothisis to hcg with further stydy, where alot if Simeons stuff is out dated, yet never dis credits Simeons. The study if Leptin only came into play i believe in 1994 so although Simeons had observed, used curcumference, and the process of elimination, and knew hcg worked, Robins info is some "more" information based on much study as well as observing over 1000s of patients, calories in calories out is no longer aplicable now that we know about Leptin. Ive read pounds and inches a few times and scanned it over a dozen, but now Robin was able to make more connections to the whys of some of these things (such as gallbladder) seeing it in her own studies with people, yet in her own words "more research is needed" So in the end since Simeons who we are all greatful for, we need to look into the newer research and see more done, Robin has had a few conversations with Dr Jack, im sure.

### JoanneA — 2012-03-21T20:30:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3981>

Colleen, as people are on protocol, and still have weight to lose, the idea behind instict eating is Leptin, the more fat a person has the more leptin, as leptin levels drop, hunger increases so the idea is to eat along with the hormone, once leptin rises hunger goes away, but as a person gets closer to normal weight im sure hunger obviously increases, so breakfast would probably happen at some point upon rising, as well more frequent hunger levels as well requiring more food, so the theory is eating with that hormone. Thats my understanding, i believe there is yet more studys and testing to be done. Wow!!!! Those are huge forums!!!!!

### JoanneA — 2012-03-21T20:34:02+0200

### colleencoble — 2012-03-21T20:58:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-3996>

I think it's fine not to weigh while on the hcg. But it's DEADLY to ignore Dr. Simeon's advice to weigh every day after you are off the hcg. I've seen people regain so fast it's dizzying. And Simeon warns against trusting how your clothes fit. It's easy to get off with a steak day if you stay on top of things but if you let them go, BAM, hello ten pounds. Or fifteen.

Breakfast is fine to skip on hcg. But not later as Doc has taught us.  I could easily skip breakfast every day but I don't now that I know how important it is.

### Raine02 — 2012-03-23T14:09:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-4913>

New Guinea Pig coming out of hiding with my plan and a few questions. I have been reading MDA and Dr. Kruse's stuff for quite some time. I started the Leptin Reset in September and I'm doing well on it, still having trouble loosing weight, but I can maintain rather well which is a first for me, so I consider that a win. I'm taking the next step. I ordered the HCG patches, they seem to work well for a majority of people.

My Plan: I'm expecting TOM next week and should have my patches by the time it's over. I want to start right after to get the most time in. I plan on eating a low fat protein breakfast and then the same for dinner, skipping lunch. I am going to stick with lean meat and veggies, reserving the handful of berries for the days I just need something else, hoping that won't happen, but you never know. I am currently preparing mentally and thinking of different situations I may encounter and how to deal with them. I figure if I have a plan I may escape unharmed. I want to get a round in before summer hits because we are avid travelers and it's easy to keep up with the Leptin reset while traveling, I can forsee that avoiding fats while traveling may become difficult. I've got at least fifty pounds to lose, but would be thrilled to be under 166 for the first time in my adult life. That seems to me my magic number I diet my way down, hit 166 and lose it.

I want this to go well and so have a few questions. I am currently taking thyroid medication, Cytomel, can I continue on this during HCG? I plan to cut out all the oil based vitamins such as A and D3 and the fish oil, but want to keep the L-Glutamin, adreanal booster, and any others that are non oil based, is this a good idea or should I stop them all? When TOM rolls around again, I stop the patches, but continue to eat on plan, correct? I am currently looking for a no oil foundation, any suggestions. I currently use make up from 100% pure, but my tinted moisturizer has oil in it. All other makeup I will discontinue.

Looking for flaws or suggestions for my plan.

Thanks,

Lori

### MaggieC — 2012-03-24T21:06:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-5522>

In thinking about P2, isn't the only thing that matters is that you keep your calories at or around 500? If we did that, no matter what we ate, we would have to burn the fat in our bodies to make up the deficit. Wouldn't eating fat (as we do eating primal) just prime our bodies to burn fat instead of sugar?

The Gods know I'm no scientist, and Simeon apparently got it wrong about our bodies needing carbs for our brains to work. I bet it would be worth a test to see if we could do 200 calories worth of protein and the rest fat to see how our bodies react. What do you guys think of that? Has anyone tried it? I'm wondering if Simeon said "NO FAT" to keep his patients from going down a slippery slope. For us it would make more sense to say "NO CARBS"!

I agree with you Colleen, I won't be eating apples and oranges if I go back on P2, I would rather have the protein. Maybe an occasional handful of raspberries, which is what I do now. I have to do blood work and see where I'm at before I think about going back on P2. If I have to then, I'll count fish oil capsules toward my calorie count. I think I'm better off with it than without it, just a hunch.

### eleveneleven — 2012-03-26T21:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-6526>

Hi! I've done one round of hCG before, but I managed to gain all the weight back (devastating, but...it happens). During my first round, I lost 15 pounds in 35 days (including the load weight, I lost about 20 lbs) which wasn't TOO great, but it got me pretty close to goal. I've started eating paleo and in accordance with the leptin reset, but since I need to lose about 30 pounds, I'm waiting for more hCG to get here and then I'm going to do a second round. I've already decided to eat only Simeon's approved foods but with leptin reset timing, and then add CT in at night after dinner. Because I'm going full paleo, I'm leaving out the fruit and the breadsticks. I know I'm most likely not going to be able to start until the first or second week of April, depending on when the hCG gets here, but this is what a day will most likely look like for me:

**Breakfast (within 30 minutes of waking):

8oz chicken breast

1.5 cup spinach

.25 cup raw kraut

_Calories: 273, Protein: 54g

**Lunch/Dinner (timing depends on hunger, but no later than 6pm):**

4oz sea bass

100g asparagus

.25 cup fermented vegetable (either a different kind of kraut or sugar-free kimchee)

Calories: 198_

TOTAL CALORIES - 470** (might be a bit higher or lower each day depending on protein and vegetables)

I'm excited to try this, truly. I might add in lunch with a few ounces of protein and another vegetable if I'm really hungry some days, but I'll never exceed 500 calories or go fewer than 4 hours between meals. Can't wait to see the results you guys have with hCG and CT, too!

However the times between bingeing became less and less until she was binge free and has been since October. She gained ten lbs on the LR and said it's the best 10 pounds she ever gained because it started her healing. She also was hungry and kept adding more fat and protein. "HCG DIETING ALERT: Eating MCT, in winter is probably not the best choice for a cold adapted mammal, because they do not help fluidity of cell membranes. This is why we see so many problems in the literature with saturated fats in humans. The results did not make sense and researchers attributed them to disease generation. They did not make sense because they were studying animals at different stages of their mammalian biochemistry. These fats also can make a cold adapted mammal gain weight when eaten off season because of this mismatch. This is a reverse analogy to the one I used in the Paleo Summit with the banana in Canada. If you want to see proof of this fat reversal ask any person who uses the HCG diet how coconut oil or palm oil work for them on protocol. In short it sucks. Why? those oils protect warm adapted mammals eating tons of carbs. This is why CO and Palm oil are tropical oils and not found in our polar regions.

When you ask a human who uses HCG for dieting, you will find they do not do well with MCT’s during their HCG protocol use for this reason. In winter, mammals prefer animal fats like ghee, tallow, lard, bacon grease, and pastured butter as the best choices. This is wired into our brain by the CD 36 receptor and the floor of the fourth ventricle in humans too. Seafood is always a good choice no matter what season we are in. Pastured meats and offal are ideal too. Evolution is dictating what we should eat not Dr. Kruse. I am merely pointing out what many in our community are blind too today. I hope to change your reality tonight with this knowledge.

HUMAN Obesity Caveat: MCT however, will help the obese human however to reverse weight loss in winter because they are a far better choice than any carbohydrate at this time. It was my number one diesel fuel I used for my own weight loss. It will also make the obese person radiate heat if they use it in fall and winter which is a good sign that they are fat burning and not sugar burning. Coconut oil and palm oil are the ideal fats for spring and summer uses for humans. This works only for reversal of a disease and not for optimal living. I have a plan for optimal living once you reverse your disease here."

As far as fats, it sounds like Dr. K doesn't know for sure - he is relying on us to be our own n=1 and try whatever we are comfortable with for eating (i.e. stick to protocol, or remove fruits and add more protein, or eat fats and protein, or eat fats, protein and veggies) and report back, but I think he is advocating the CT for all of us regardless of what weeat(except if you're eating fruit - that wouldbe a mismatch). That's the way I a understanding it. I don't know if that answers any questions or not!

I am going to load this weekend and begin my first ever round, so my plan is protein and veggies, no fat and CT. Since I don't have any prior experience with it, to me this sounds the safest - I NEED to lose fat ASAP and if there's a chance I won't lose as much by eating fat, I will cut it out and transition back to paleo after. For those who have done hCG before, trying the keto+fat they can compare this round to previous rounds.

Suzie, I don't think it is a problem freezing your hcg. I always froze mine since I would buy the 5000iu and use for 40 days. This round I have my syringes filled for 20 days and froze them. I take it out then night before for the next day use and put in fridge to thaw. It has not lost it's potency. So...go ahead and use what you have with you.

Also, just get right back on and eat VLC. You may gain a pound or few  in the next couple days, or not. Or you may stall. You never know with a cheat.

Good luck!

Sorry friends, I'm traveling and just got online after 2 days on the road. Heading home to Indiana from AZ.  You're fine, Suzie. Some hcgers actually freeze theirs. Don't panic.

And shinegirl, much of your weight loss this week is likely that 10 pounds falling off quickly and water weight from your period. It usually does. I hope your losses with coconut oil continue but I'm leery. LOL We'll see. Fats of any kind tend to be problematic for stabilization. I hope CT makes stabilization a breeze but this is an experiment. You won't know how this worked for a good 6 months, maybe even a year. I think we're going to see better stabilization in general and more fat lost as well with CT. ### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T05:41:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9103>

Well, it's pretty clear from Doc's post when he was asking for questions about CT that no one really knows what is best with hcg and CT. He's looking to us to prove or disprove whether adding fat is okay or not. So I'm going to experiment and add coconut oil. The worst that can happen is I'll regain the weight in a few months. I have enough hcg to do another round if I have to. I'm leery but it's not fatal.  And I've done plenty of rounds so I'll know whether it works or not by how I stabilize because I've always done okay. Steak days always drop me down and I settle right where I should. So I'll be able to tell for sure if the fat messes with stabilization even with CT added.

I'm the type that hcg was designed for--one with a broken metabolism who can't lose any other way. One who will gain or stall with the least change to the protocol. Some can lose with adding different vegetables or different proteins but I never have been able to vary it AT ALL. So we need people like me to test it. Younger ones with better metabolisms have often been able to very it some. We broken ones have to figure this out for others.

I'm scared. LOL

### MaggieC — 2012-03-31T10:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9141>

Tomorrow is vlcd1 for me. I'm aiming for 60% P, 25% fat, and 15% carbs and 500 calories. That lets me get in a breakfast with 25g protein as well as lunch and dinner if I go with lean foods. I did blood work 2 days ago so when I get those results I'll post them. I did a Leptin Reset for a little over 6 weeks.

I'm also a broken metabolism person. I manage to get down to about 143 lbs. and then I stall... and bounce back up. When I try to go back to "regular" eating (P3) my weight drifts back up to the 150s. So this time, no fruit, no dairy, obviously no wheat. LR timing, and a strict 500 calories with no coconut oil. I also planned to throw in CT as well, and I'm already freezing! I will have to start with spot icing I think on my belly and hips.

Yes, I also keep my shots frozen. They keep much longer that way. I usually do 200 mg shots, and this round I'm trying 175 mg. Thank you Colleen! I'm also a bit scared in a way. I'm tired of "failing" with hcg. This round I want to drop 25 lbs and get to where I want to be. Then I'll move on to post leptin reset eating to stabilize. If I can stick to LR eating for 6 weeks, 6 weeks on hcg will be a breeze!

### Honey Crisp — 2012-03-31T13:08:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9151>

I went rogue in previous rounds with added fats and didn't run into problems during P2. P3, I could eat 1800 calories with my ratios around 60 fat/25 protein/15 carbs and still lose weight. I had to limit nuts/berries/cheese to about 1 oz servings a day. The trouble was during P4, I'd add grains or sugar and the weight would sky rocket. It's the reason I turned to the paleo/primal lifestyle.

### Pamela — 2012-03-31T23:47:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9398>

I have a question for people here. Why would you add fat while you are on the HCG protocol? The original document by dr Simeon as well as so many people saying that any cheat will not only cause a gain but give problems stabilizing. Even adding CT to the HCG protocol seems off considering Dr K says to eat a high fat meal prior to CT.

I will say what my experiment is. I just finished a round that I did by the Dr S book. Did it for 30 days lost 16 lbs and am now at 161 at 5'8" Had very little fruit and no grassini. The last round I did was in early fall and after 6-8 weeks I was not able to stabilize. Weight started creeping back on. This time I am changing a few things. This last round was done in winter which could be a better time. 1) CT 3-5 times per week with cold baths. 2) no eggs since they can cause inflamation with Hashimotos and I did not know this on last round. 3) no dairy 4) staying ketogenic for duration of CT by testing each morning 5) doing Leptin reset protocol.

I will do the above for 8 weeks and see how I stabilize. But this is after I got the weight off. I personally would not recommend adding fat while taking HCG.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-01T02:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9561>

I need some help, desperately.

I am in R2P2. My first round was great and I did well stabilizing in P3 by using paleo/LR. Total losses from R1 25 from hCG and 5 from paleo/LR.

I started loading last Saturday. On day one I lost a pound. On day two of loading I gained 1.5. After one week I have only lost my load weight and .4! I loaded well with good fats, no sugar, no starch, no grains.

R1 I did some off protocol, using Walden Farms products, did some coco delite, and even used CO to stir fry chicken and shrimp.

VLCD day one I gained .4! Day 2 no loss so I dropped using the WF salad dressing. Day 3 no loss so I dropped one of the fruits. Day 4 no loss so I dropped the other fruit.

Day 5 no loss so I dropped one veggie and added some more protein. Day 6 no loss so I added more protein eating only one veggie and no fruits, no off protocol stuff. This am the .4 loss plus the load weight loss. Crap.

I am stumped. Obviously what worked in R1 isn’t gonna work in R2. I am planning a 40 day round. I need some advice.

I have not had beef, just chicken and shrimp. I did make chicken cabbage soup with my homemade bone broth. I only eat cabbage, lettuce, celery, tomatoes and onions since I can’t gag down cucumbers, greens, chard, spinach, fennel or asparagus. (Don’t condemn me, blame my mother who served such stuff when I was a kid and it was usually canned crap with tons of sodium and no flavor. Ick,Ick,Ick,) Not had any bathroom issues, everything’s fine.

Anyone have any thoughts? I know the hcg is good, expiration date is 9/2014 and it is RX Gonakor from Mexico, and I mixed it correctly. I am not hungry but feel the need to eat at normal lunch and dinner times.

I am perplexed to say the least. I am stubborn and refuse to give up.

Any insight (or even a hang in there!) is greatly appreciated.

dennise

I am doing the Hcg with the CT. Not adding in the CO. Just not eating the fruit or the grains this round. My losses are about the same as when I did the protocol last year. > I have not had beef, just chicken and shrimp. I did make chicken cabbage soup with my homemade bone broth. I only eat cabbage, lettuce, celery, tomatoes and onions since I canâ€™t gag down cucumbers, greens, chard, spinach, fennel or asparagus. (Donâ€™t condemn me, blame my mother who served such stuff when I was a kid and it was usually canned crap with tons of sodium and no flavor. Ick,Ick,Ick,) Not had any bathroom issues, everythingâ€™s fine.
>
>
>
> Anyone have any thoughts? I know the hcg is good, expiration date is 9/2014 and it is RX Gonakor from Mexico, and I mixed it correctly. I am not hungry but feel the need to eat at normal lunch and dinner times.

### shilohman — 2012-04-01T06:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9706>

> **rocketmom;9753 wrote:** Bumping to see if anyone has some suggestions...

You have to lose the chicken from your diet. Chickens are fed corn/soy pellets, so their meat is very high in omega 6. Have you had your omega 6/3 ratio tested? As long as you are eating any food high in omega 6 you are going to gain weight. This is why Dr K recommends only red grass fed meat.

### Honey Crisp — 2012-04-01T12:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9729>

> **rocketmom;9753 wrote:** ...I am perplexed to say the least. I am stubborn and refuse to give up.
>
>
>
> Any insight (or even a hang in there!) is greatly appreciated.

What type of diet were you eating before protocol? If it was a paleo/primal I don't think you'll see a big drop from water weight like you do going from SAD to Hcg Protocol.

How much weight do you need/want to lose? I'm in my healthy weight range and the losses are usually slow for me at this point.

I think you did a great job in ditching the Walden Farm products. It's frankenfood. You are healing your body with good food and adding the frankenfood cancels it.

### texasmom4 — 2012-04-01T15:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9744>

> **shilohman;9768 wrote:** You have to lose the chicken from your diet. Chickens are fed corn/soy pellets, so their meat is very high in omega 6. Have you had your omega 6/3 ratio tested? As long as you are eating any food high in omega 6 you are going to gain weight. This is why Dr K recommends only red grass fed meat.

Chicken is "on protocol" per Dr Simeon, but Shilohman has a good point if you are trying to marry Dr K's advice with HCG. FWIW, I did gain/stall out every time I ate chicken during round 1 and 2. Never connected the dots until I started round 3 though. I only ate deer meat, fish, and an occasional beef sirloin my entire round 3 and had really great losses.

Have you scrutinized all of your facial products/make-up? I lost less when I weighed Monday mornings and finally figured out my "oil free" foundation that I put on for church on Sunday was the problem.

I'm sorry you are having trouble. At least you aren't hungry - that would indicate a big problem. August may be right - you may just have to ride it out. I hope the scale starts moving for you soon!

Amanda

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-01T16:23:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9761>

I did it! I took the challenge and I lost. On Friday I had not planned well for a shopping trip and found myself at a Mexican restaurant hungry. I order a house salad with dressing on the side and my husband had fajita. I took some of the fajita chicken and onions from him and “dipped” a little dressing. I also could not resist a few chips and salsa. Then what the heck a square of dark chocolate. The rest of the day was as Simeon instructions.

I lost 1.6 lbs wow I thought I had blown it.

Saturday I had bacon for breakfast 40min in my pool at 70 degrees. Regular Dr. S rest of day except the on dark chocolate square.

Then 25 min in tub at 60 degree last night. This morning I lost 1 lb. I ate more protein and fat yesterday and did not experience the hunger that I have been fighting while on Simeon protocol.

I have lost 7.8 lbs. in 7 days that includes my load weight.

Just had my bacon headed to cold tub and will grill grass fed beef today. Can we do the BAB on this? Dare I try?

Anyone else trying fat? I sure had missed bacon and could see my skin was getting very dry.

I will update you as I go.

### indigogirl — 2012-04-01T17:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9769>

I am eating bacon for bkfst...40% fat reduced and losing really well. Ate this bacon on my first round and also lost really well.

I also use a Clinique eye cream day and night and a dermalogica lotion to calm my roseacea in the morning.

My hands are very dry from the lack of fat and I use a hand lotion frequently throughout the day.

Otherwise following Simeon's protocol.

I am CTing very sporadically (face dunks) and would like to increase this, but am traveling a lot in April and will see how I can fit it in.

I'm on day 23 and have lost 16.5 lbs, and plan on staying on HCG for 40 days.

I eat chicken, but only from Whole Foods...which is organic, but I don't think it's pastured.

I will stabilize slowly over a course of 6 weeks, and last time I continued to lose weight over stabilization, about another 6 lbs. For a total of 42 lbs. During stabilization I still didn't eat carbs and only low sugar fruits (berries, apples, grapefruit). I did eat eggs, yogurt, and nuts. Might not add back eggs this time (I have hashimotos), but think it will be really hard to not eat eggs due to their convenience.

I maintained my weight for about 6 months, started Paleo, tried the Leptin Reset twice and each time cheated and ended up completely blowing everything, ate crap and gained everything back over about 6 months.

I think we are all so individual!

I look forward to following everyone's experiments.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-01T19:13:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9831>

Thanks everyone for such great input. I really appreciate all the info.

I am 59 so TOM is not an issues. I did stabilize on P3/P4 using Paleo/LR. More paleo than LR since the BAB was difficult for me. I realize that I haven't been eating grains, legumes, sugar, starch and lots of dairy since I started Jan 12 on hCG, so my great loss in week one of vlcd was the elimination of those food groups. I do buy chicken at the local grocery and they probably are fed grain etc. I buy organic grass fed beef but have shied away from beef my first week. Weighing in this am I am down 3.5 lbs and that averages to .5 which is what I am aiming for this 40 day round. I have yet to start CT but the more I read about it the better it looks, too bad I am such a coward! Cabbage being a reason for stalling breaks my heart, as I used it alot through all the phases of R1. I tossed all the Walden Farms stuff(when hubby was out walking the dogs...just what he needed to see...wasting $...) I will get my chicken from Sprouts where I get the organic grass fed beef tomorrow.

I will attempt to do CT this evening. We are on our way to South Padre Island to celebrate my FIL's 95th birthday. We will be eating out...not me that is. But they do have boiled peel and eat shrimp and salad so I may be able to finagle something but will bring my cooler bag with me just in case. I will bring my own homemade salad dressing too.

Thanks again everyone...you are a great group. So glad to be a part of it.

dennise

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T00:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10006>

The bummer is that hcg has gotten a little tougher to get than it was 2 mos ago. But you can still get it at [www.reliablerx.com](http://http://www.reliablerx.com). You need either 1 5000 iu vial (could just barely squeak through with this on a 30 day round) or better yet 2 2000 iu vials. You can get the bac water on Amazon. You'll need a mixing needle for each vial. I buy the 18 gauge. And you'll need insulin needles which you can get at gpzservices.com. If you buy 2000 iu vials you won't need sterile mixing vials--you can add the vac water to the hcg vial.

I did all my rounds while on the Dzugan method. It really doesn't matter whether you're on it or not. Hcg is sooo great! It works better than you can imagine.  If you go to my website ([www.colleencoble.com](http://http://www.colleencoble.com)) go to Extras and there is a page for hcg. There are links there for the syringes. The links for hcg don't work now because All Day Chemist isn't selling it. Any of the hcg brands are good.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T03:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10097>

You don't need mixing vials if you get the 2000 iu. But yes you will need the syringes to go with the needles.  and bac water and needles look good.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-02T03:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10099>

> **nuttmegs17;10148 wrote:** so everything else looks ok?
>
>
>
> colleen said: "If you buy 2000 iu vials you won't need sterile mixing vials--you can add the vac water to the hcg vial. "
>
>
>
> is that not true?
>
>
>
>
>
> thank you!

Oh sorry, I see you are mixing the 2000iu. So if you are dosing 125iu/day (which is what Dr S protocol recommends) you will need 8cc of bac water to mix. That should give you 16 days. You will need the dispensing vials....[at least the 10cc size](http://http://www.gpzservices.com/items/products/vials%7Epresealed/-br%7Ebr-amber/10-ml-presealed-vial-amber-10mlap-detail.htm). You will add 1cc of bac water into the vial with the hcg pill to dissolve. Then use the syringe to take the 1cc dissolved hcg and gently add to the dispenser amber vial, then add your remaining 7cc of bac water to get your 8cc total. That is what you will draw from everyday.

Connie will chime in if need to correct this.

### Shinegirl — 2012-04-02T16:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10280>

Good morning everyone! I hope everyone is feeling amazing today. I only got 5 hrs sleep but when I did close my eyes I was completely out. Slept so well. I'm still a little tired though. So, funny story. Kinda.

I was determined to get my CT yesterday after my lunch and dinner but I had to work around a few challenges. I was vacationing with my in-laws and my husbands mother has MS. She drinks iced tea like crazy so was not about to steal all her ice for my CT bath.

Cold shower is just not enough for me so I decided to try something that I thought was pretty clever.

Everyone knows what thermocline is right?

Well, I'm in Texas, so the 1st foot of our lake is pretty much lukewarm water right now. However, if you reach down about 2 1/2 ft you can stir up some 55 - 60 degree water. Perfect for growing fishies like me.

So long story short... I asked my husband how ling I was out there swimming underwater. My goal was 25 to 30 minutes, which is my average CT bath. Well, he told me I swam for an hour and a half which is ridiculous because I'm such a "floater".

Long story shorter:

I gained .4 lbs

I feel incredibly balanced. This mornings yoga was a breeze. No tightness.

Slept well

Lost 1/2 inch off waist

Lunch: 1 can tuna with egg and mayo on sliced zuccini

Coco manna

Dinner: 1 chicken breast salad with 1 egg and mayo

Coco manna

Coco manna

### Marilee0123 — 2012-04-02T17:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10305>

Hello everyone! I thought I would jump in here and join you. I'm on R2P2D43 of Hcg with a loss of 33.8lbs. That is a typical loss for me for 40 days. I follow Simeon's protocol. For the past 2 weeks I've incorporated CT. My losses on week 4 (1st week of CT showed a gain of 2lbs) NEVER had a weekly gain on Hcg. On week 5 I has a total .8 loss for the week. I dropped the fruit (strawberries), continued the CT and had a 4lb loss in one day. I am continuing to drop steady as I normally do. Here's some background info on me....I found Hcg in Feb 2011. I started at 335lbs, injected continually for 7 months from the end Feb '11 until the end of Sept '11. I showed no immunity and I took a few PI's (2-3) for myself and my family. In Sept my LIW was 205. I maintained until the end of Dec and I started to slowly gain 10 lbs without changing my eating habits of maintenance. I could not correct back to 205 and stay there. It was very discouraging. I found LeptinRx in Jan and I was able to maintain at 215. I started this round on Feb 20 at 235. That included a 20lb load weight...ugh...which came off the first few days, whew! Today I'm sitting at 198.4. I will continue like I did last time until I reach a weight that I feel comfortable with (still haven't decided what that should be!). I belong to all the BIG boards and even after a year or more, I continue to read and learn something new everyday. I felt that I could hopefully help someone else and also benefit myself the most from this forum. Happy Losing!

Marilee

### challdo — 2012-04-02T19:03:21+0300

> **RBee;10402 wrote:** oh, and gym pants....with my sweatshirt LOL

Rbee-clothes are optional only in some areas of the world!

Hey everyone,

I am 52yo female who is a practioner and patient of hCG. I have over 1000 patients who have done the diet. P3/4 throws everyone for a loop and now I am asking all to do Leptin Rx for phase 3/4.

My last round (#?) I did Leptin Rx for Phase 3/4 and stablized like a rock but never lost weight on it or on post Leptin protocol. Been doing CT 4-7 days a week in 48 degree water for a 1/2 hour or more.

Yesterday I started again-a 20 day round-I will modify this with Keto diet-meat only with breakfast and dinner. I will do all of the supplements Doc has indicated. I am still debating adding coconut oil/MCT.

Will report stats in the a.m. as to post load numbers.

I'm tired of this and want to get my weight down for good. Leptin resistance is the missing link. Lifestyle modification is required and will be embraced.

Watched my labs-hsCRP was elevated immediately post hCG Phase 2-then was able to cut it in half with supplements and Leptin Rx but couldnt get it lower. Thyroid ratio was 80! Boy, was I happy to see this as I take iodine and Armour thyroid daily. DHEAS was >200. Pregnenalone was low. Currently taking progesterone sublingual, Pregnenalone, DHEA, Testosterone. No Estrogen as still having periods. Taking almost all supplements for Leptin Rx too-but these stop tomorrow as I start hCG VLCD in a.m. and will switch to those suggested by Dr Kruse.

Also, I agree with Jack, hCG works best for the obese patient. Losses of 25-34 lbs are common on a 40 day round in my practice. YMMV-(your mileage may vary)

Looking for support as this is very off protocol, and like Colleen, I was very Dr.S. indoctrinated.

Cindy

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T19:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10385>

The Corion vial is actually a little larger then the hucog vial. And both will allow a total of 4 cc of fluid in a 2000 iu vial which is a good mixing dose.  You can't do it with a 5000 iu vial though. I don't like a super potent mix.

All things considered, I view this as a success. I'll be cutting out the non-paleo items this week and see if the results are any better!

### MaggieC — 2012-04-02T22:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10464>

The trick to weighing in every day is to not get attached to the weight. Some weeks will move nicely, and some just won't. The theory is that the empty fat cells are filling up with water before they finally let go I believe... Someone on another forum is tracking her body composition numbers to prove that theory... it's fascinating stuff. Our bodies are complicated organisms that have to do things their way. Do your best to look at it from a scientific/experimental point of view and you'll be much happier. You can't beat yourself up because you aren't losing a pound a day! That's a waste of energy and a spike in your cortisol levels!

Weighing daily gives you good data if you're tracking your food intake. I use perfect diet tracker for instance. That can help you figure out if you have food sensitivities. It's all just data!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T23:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10491>

I like weighing every day but it's not going to hurt if you just don't want to do that. But you need to weigh every day in P3 so you can take action with a steak day when it's needed.

Do your best to look at it from a scientific/experimental point of view and you'll be much happier. You can't beat yourself up because you aren't losing a pound a day! That's a waste of energy and a spike in your cortisol levels!

Weighing daily gives you good data if you're tracking your food intake. I use perfect diet tracker for instance. That can help you figure out if you have food sensitivities. It's all just data!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-03T15:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10744>

nutmeggs, I don't do anything fancy while on hcg so I can't really help you with menus. I eat very plainly so I can break the bond to food. Boredom is good while on the protocol. LOL I don't eat out if I can help it while on the protocol. Actually I don't eat out nearly as much as I used to in general because you just can't trust what they put in your food. If I do have to eat out, I order fish and broccoli because if a tiny bit of fat makes its way to the fish, it's still lower in fat and calories than any other choice.

Joanne, you might try just icing your back instead of submersion if you can't stand it. And I wear socks to bed. LOL

shinegirl, now is where the truth will come out about whether you will lose well with the modified diet. You can't trust the first week, especially with gaining a lot on load. That flies off in the first week and if you gained more than usual, that will account for higher than usual numbers. Hope the losses pick back up though! But the next two weeks will show us more than anything else will.

I always have 10 day stalls in the middle of my round. I'm a slow loser too, about .5 a day average. I'm hoping the elimination of fruit will help that but we'll see. Hcg is so tricky. LOL

Colleen, This may not be the place to post this but it seems you look at this thread a lot so I chose it. Would you tell again what you did for food sensitivities or tell me where to find the info? I know I read it somewhere but can't find it. I have many food allergies and need all the help I can get.

Started hhcg for vanity and then realized how much dropping wheat and milk did for my stuffy nose. I also have osteoporosis and Eosiniphilic Esophagitis I know LR Rx will help. I lost 28 pounds on two rounds and for the second round went into Leptin Rx for p3. I have continued to lose slowly, 5.6 pounds in 6 weeks. I also am doing the leaky gut Rx. but I am not sure how to know if one's leaky gut is healed.

I haven't started the CT. My mom died about the time I started P3 (a blessing as she suffered from osteoporosis and other neolithic disease that had completely debilitated her for years - but still a lot of stress and hectic schedule for me) which slowed down my progressing to CT. I also live in the sticks and am still waiting for the thermometer to arrive. Which reminds me, Colleen, where do you buy a laser for the desensitizing?

I have about 15 pounds to lose to get to a 19 BMI and as I am losing slowly still I am reticent to do CT. Will it hurt in your opinion to continue a keto diet without CT? Dr. K has said the vagus may be damaged in the Nissan procedure I had for reflux but as I am contining to lose I am hoping that is not so. I do have more lower body weight to lose than upper though so probably CT, especially lower body, would be helpful.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-05T20:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-11823>

> **Zoe Patricia;11772 wrote:**
>
> Also he suggested bone broth (some where in there) but that is not really on protocol. The new Atkins book says broth clears up induction flu in a flash so I am soaking on it. Wish there was a way to make broth on protocol.
>
> Laughter is supposed to help cortisol which really grabbed me. Definitely going in my first aid kit.

Patricia, I make my own bone broth from chicken bones. I buy the bone in skin on breasts and debone, defat and throw the skin away. I plop them babies in my crock pot, add some water, garlic powder, onion powder and maybe some celery tops if I have them and let it cook away. Usually overnight. It makes awesome stock. You can add salt to your liking. Much better than any low sodium stuff in a box or can. Strain out the bones and the little bit of chicken meat, and the celery tops and then stick in the fridge. Skim off any fat that remains to defat it. I think someone on another board mentioned that homemade stock was about 5 calories a cup. I also do beef bone stock, the same way. It rocks. Ask the meat guy for soup bones. There are some hcg recipes online about making chicken stock from your daily protein allowance, but I find that after cooking in water the chicken is really awful.

dennise

### MartiD — 2012-04-05T22:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-11867>

Ok, I have read all through this thread and wanted to stop in for a quick hello. My background is pretty easy, I started with round 1 last June and lost 28lbs, did the reset in between rounds and stayed about 2lbs under LDW. Did a second round in the fall, shorter round (24 day), immediately went in to LeptinRX mid October and then dropped another 10 lbs til I started CT in February. In the last month or so I have lost another 3 lbs. My original goal was 195 from 228lbs. After first round success I adjusted to 185 for second round. I finished round 2 at 187 and today I am at 173. I would like to now go to 160-165. This would put me in a very comfortable size 10 and even though according to the BMI chart, which I know doesn't have much credibility, I still would be consider a healthy weight but I would be happy!

So, I am debating two options, stick with LeptinRX plus CT and see what happens with the warmer months coming up or finish out a bottle of HHCG pellets I have from last fall, try to go at least 21 days and add CT, then immediately fall back into LeptinRX. I never had an issue with stabilizing, never went 2lbs over LDW either round and according to my labs last fall I am not out of whack on my system.

Thoughts? I guess my biggest fear is messing up a good thing. I know how lucky I am that I have dropped more after rounds on Leptin and CT. Am I being greedy?

Marilee, I'm super shocked that you are seeing gains... I sure hope someone has an answer for you on this.

I still haven't 100% abandoned the calorie consideration in dieting, though I don't believe it's as simple as a lot of folks make it seem, but generally speaking, 500 calories should result in a noticeable drop on the scale

I do find it a relief to not wake up every morning and immediately jump on the scale. It's helped my stress a lot, lol

ETA: I just had a thought... have you been doing CT for awhile? It's supposed to release hormones from your fat stores. If you have just started CT you may still be releasing lots of estrogen from your stored fat, and I can definitely see a gain with high estrogen levels...
