### Slone07 — 2012-04-06T16:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-12120>

I've been bitten by the hCG bug again (thanks to y'all).

My plan was to do the full leptin reset (no CT for me yet) to see where it brought me in eight to ten weeks (I'm at two and a half weeks), but with it being the start of summer here in TX and me being so darn huge (read: not fitting into anything but my winter clothes), I was losing motivation quickly and it was causing more stress than I wanted to live with. I see the leptin reset as a major compliment to hCG, so I'm excited to apply it to my rounds, do it between rounds, and longer term after I'm at a healthier weight. So far, the LR has felt great, but with me having Hashi's, adrenal fatigue, and who knows what else until I get some labs, it could take a very, very long time for the LR to work...and my heart hurts when I think of all the time I'm wasting, wasting my life. HCG has worked beautifully for me in the past (30+ pounds in as many days) and I was one of the "roguers" over on the big hCG forum, although I gained it back on and after a vacation (vacation is my downfall - especially when we visit family for a few weeks in New Orleans - must.stay.away. from the pasta and po' boys!).

Anyway, I'm really excited to have a game plan that works for me now. I feel like I have a clear map to getting healthy and feeling great now. That, in and of itself, feels great.

> Chimpchick - were you doing CTing a lot before starting HCG? do you think you were cold adapted before hcg started? perhaps that is why you are by-passing the detox stage that many others seem to be experiencing which in turns make HCG + ct so hard to manage together

Hey Meg!

I had already started HCG when CT came to be.. I was also playing with Fat on and off before I started CT. I noticed when I combined fat and CT my losses were super. I haven't had any increased hunger since starting CT either. I also am only doing face dunks and ice packs. (2-3 hrs per day) Lots of surface area.. I don't have a tub, but soon will have a chilly pool I am 'sorta' looking fwd too

The only detox symptoms I noticed were like 2 pimples and my TOM came early while on the pill.. Other then that I haven't noticed anything in a while..

I have been following the Fat Flush HCG thread over at low carb friends.. There are a bunch of people having great success combining fat and hcg.. Take a look.. TOM is arriving with the Easter Bunny so I am boycotting the scale as well for a few days. I can feel I am still losing. Next week I will be done with HCG and moving into the leptin reset + CT.. I am also going to incorporate JUDDD maintenance rules and/or (if I need a correction day- or want to lose more) JUDDD weight loss rules. It seems like the only way to do the reset and still be able to correct, as you can't do a steak day on the reset..

I wish you all feel better! Maybe just stick to face dunks and spot icing while oh HCG? I donno? Maybe I would have more of these symptoms if I had a tub.. Probably...

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-10T03:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13199>

nuttmegs, I think Connie stated that you wouldn't need the storage vial if you are mixing the bac water right into the pill vial. You may want to cover the vial once mixed with foil to store in the refrigerator. If you feel better using the storage vial, yes, I would get the amber ones to keep the mixed hcg protected from light. HTh, and if you need any help with mixing lmk.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-10T05:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13233>

You would mix 4 cc of fluid in that vial, meggs. If it's a hucog vial, that fills it clear to the brim. If it's Corian, you will have a bit of room at the top. No need to get a sterile vial unless you want to mix it more dilute. I would start at 30 units which is 150 iu but you may need to go down a bit from there but we'll see.

### Zoe Patricia — 2012-04-11T21:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13846>

Sorry about your aunt Colleen.

Checking in here. I am on day six and cannot get the dose correct. I have been doing CT now since early February and am doing 30 to 45 minutes once a day in ice tub. Round one I lost 28 pounds, I started CT the last week of round one. I did the reset between round one and this round. Followed it carefully with daily CT and gained 5 pounds over the six weeks. This round I am just trying to make it work at all. Here is what info I have. I am taking hucog 2,000 in 8cc bac water. I started by injection 130, then as I was hungry I brought it down to 125 (50 on insulin syringe) then to 120 for 2 days. I did good the first day on 120 but the second day on 120 I was uncomfortable with hunger late in the day and could not sleep. This morning I went back up to 123.

The CT definitely reacts for me with the HCG. I am sensitive enough to get that big time. I seem great before the CT, the hunger comes like a storm after the CT.

Tomorrow is my skip day. Should I skip? Then what dose? I am aiming for 30 days and I am not in harmony yet. My gut feeling is to stay at 120 and split into 2 injections and not skip. I am hoping for 30 days.

I am considering 2 injections a day because I feel completely different before and after the afternoon CT.

I keep thinking after the CT , hey what happened to the hcg that was on board... HCG is supposed to have a 33 hour half life but wow it really feels to me like the CT burns it off. It feels like I am just on low calorie with no hcg.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-12T02:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13997>

Need some help!

Started HCG SubQ injections on Saturday, today was VLCD3. Dose @ 175 iu Hucog.

I am eating no fruit or melba. Food looks like this:

BAB: 2 servings protein, 1 serving veggie

LL (late lunch, trying to stretch to dinner only eventually): 1 protein, 1/2 veg

DIN: 1 protein, 1/2 veg to bring me to 500 cals (no less than 450)

My problem is approx 2. hours after the LL, I start having \*intense\* cravings. I mean -- wanting to dip my kid's Cheez Its into ranch dressing, snarf the jelly beans from easter, and polish it off with a chocolate bar -- kind of cravings. All foods I don't care for, pass up all the time, no big deal.

I am not hungry the rest of the day, except for the, "Hmm, I think it's time to eat some lunch now." type, which isn't abnormal. I'm fine after eating. It just seems to be about 3:30-4pm in the afternoon.

SO what is causing this? It's only been these 3 VLCD days... and it doesn't seem to pass if I eat an early dinner. I though perhaps I was just hungry. Any ideas??? It's truly driving me nuts. I nearly face-planted into the food trough this afternoon, it was a close, close, close call. I can't go 40 more days battling this!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T06:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-14099>

Thanks for the hugs, friends! I'm still in Phoenix, heading home on Friday.

Zoe, I would still skip. Since this is not your first round, it's possible you have to do down to about 110 or so. Maybe not but that's a surefire way to find out!

shinegirl, it's impossible to say if you'll have a problem with the leptin reset, but one thing I've seen is that those who don't eat eggs have a better chance at not gaining at the beginning. I'm not sure if that's because eggs are inflammatory or if there are some underlying egg sensitivity issues. But try at least the first week of P3 without eggs if you want to give yourself the best possible chance.

That's a high dose, Jeepifer. I'd skip your shot tomorrow and see how you feel. I bet you'll start feeling great by evening. I never like to see people start higher than 150. And even then, many need to take it down a line or so on the syringe.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-14T01:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-14939>

Zoe, if you mixed it with 8 cc of fluid, 25 is only 75 iu. You're taking WAY too little. Are you sure you mixed 8 cc in the 2000 iu? 25 is usually 125 iu if mixed with 4 cc.

Justfourme, I don't know what is happening yet with the CT. Doc has said the hcg sets the body up to expect cold so when we do CTing it should help with losses and help cement them. But there seems to be some problems with some. Not sure what it's all about yet. It's too soon to know.

### Zoe Patricia — 2012-04-14T05:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-15001>

I mixed 2,000 with 8 cc. I am pretty lost. I started at 55, then went to 50 (125 iu) then went lower to 40 (120 iu.) Then skip day ,then wrong dose (75), i meant to do 110 but miscalculated. I am not sure what to take next. You said I might need to go as low as 110. Obviously I have made a mistake and went too low. Bad brain fog. I am way way off protocol.

So a new day.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-14T17:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-15054>

You felt fine on skip day so it's clear you needed a slightly smaller dose. Try doing 35 units for a couple of days. But if the hunger doesn't go away after that, you need to stop because the hcg isn't working for you.

### Claudia — 2012-04-15T18:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-15325>

I just started my hcg round and my first doing injections, today is day 3 loading day, so tomorrow will start with vld. I'm confused by what I just read about CT causing problems. I've been doing CT for awhile now and I am up do cold baths a few times per week for 30 minutes. Should I be stopping the CT? I was planning on continuing a couple times per week with the cold baths and then, when done with hcg, start cranking up the CT again to more days per week.

I mixed 5000 iu with 10 ml of bacteriostatic water and I am injecting 25 for a dose of 125. So far it's not bad. I had trouble yesterday. Do I turn the vial I am withdrawing the hcg from upside down? I always add 25 of air into the vial and then, withdraw 25 from it to inject. Yesterday, it seemed like there wasn't enough and there was air at the top closer to the needle so I keep withdrawing more and then pushed it down. I was nervous that I was doing it wrong.

I am going to be strict on this protocol and I am not adding any experimental coconut oil or anything else. No fats, no oils for the round, which is will probably be around 28 days since I am going to go right through my next TOM.

Thanks for any advice.

### Zoe Patricia — 2012-04-16T17:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-15592>

This past week, even with all my problems getting started, my shape has changed. I had the unfortunate shape of a water tower. Now getting more feminine. It is a marvel. This is happening rapidly with HCG and at least 30 minutes a day in ice tub. I did not get this kind of shape shifting with just CT and paleo. Or with years of personal training and primal eating. I have done CT and paleo almost daily since early February. Also because of hunger and adjusting to the HCG I have not gotten the 500 calorie food protocol yet at all so it has been like hcg and paleo and CT....

I am hoping to get that piece in place this week.

It took me every bit of a week last round to get the dose right. Yesterday at 2,000 in 8 bac water, I dosed at 35. I was hungry late evening but not crazy. This morning I bumped it up to 40. (120) I sailed through yesterday and so far today I feel great. When I start to feel unbalanced fats help a lot. I sure am tempted to just add some fat. Maybe a small can of vital choice salmon.

I have a low tolerance for hunger , a souvenir of anorexia I guess even though I have been recovered 30 years. I did well last round once I got the dose right (thank you Coleen) and I have kept it off. I am pretty sure the dose is right now.

### LinD — 2012-04-19T05:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-16464>

> **colleencoble;15555 wrote:** I'd try the CT with hcg and see how you do. That's what I'm going to do.

Colleen, I thought you were done and not doing any more hCG?

Since about January, about six weeks after ending the second round, I am up almost 6# from LIW. I wonder if I went too low?... 5'7" & 126 LIW... ?? After all, the second round was more of an experiment to see if my back pain would go away like it did with the first (long) round (it did!) and resolve a suspected MS symptom (it did not). If I lost more on the second short round (30 days), that would work for me since I wanted to lose a bit more. So I did and really liked the result.

I've been doing CT since about Feb, I believe. Now, our Texas water is quite warm and I still haven't been able to get it to 50/55, but do what I can. Spot icing here and there... I find I sleep better when I start with a frozen pack under my chin/neck area at night. LOL. I'm so strange these days!

Since I'm long waisted, I can easily hide the weight so concerned this gain, if it increases, will require another round of hCG before long. Then I wonder if I have mental issues, but I enjoyed 126#. Hard to describe why, but it felt like such an accomplishment and felt better.

I've been eating protein and more protein, plus about 14 pieces of macadamia nuts... Then I discovered 99% chocolate. Rarely any veggies. I mean rarely. Guess it's the time of year to start veggies again... But with the weight gains, I'm hesitant to do so. I had a fruit on Saturday, so that was the first tine since December? I forget when I started the winter eating plan Kruse has been teaching us.

So for now, I'll see how it goes.... My neurologist was unwilling late last year to order some of the labs to optimize my health, so not sure who to see at this point for labs.

But I would do hCG again. It's probably too soon at the moment, plus DH might have me committed if I do. He likes nee the eat I am, but he liked me before I lost 40+#.

Following this thread to see how y'all are doing. (oh, and CT is addictive!)

Sprint Samsung Epic

### colleencoble — 2012-04-20T04:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-16884>

You could skip anytime after you have had 4 shots. This is your first VLCD though so I would wait another day and see how you do. If you'd had hunger earlier in the day, I would be suspicious but I think it's just the first day minor twinges.

I shouldn't lose slower. That's really kind of a fallacy with hcg. Most people I see on the protocol who want to lose 15 pounds lost them in 2 weeks. Not to lose a pound on the very first day is very suspicious. The scale could be tricking me tonight so we'll see what tomorrow brings. And even if I don't lose, I'll give it a few more days before I conclude anything.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-21T18:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17450>

At one point he suggested rather than coconut oil, we try butter or cream. I don't believe the hcg diet and dairy don't go together. It's the FAT that doesn't work. The protocol allows 1T of milk. And the vegetarians on the protocol often use whey protein or yogurt and low fat cottage cheese and lose.

I just tried it as an experiment for Doc. I am the type of person the protocol was written for so if it worked for me, it would work for just about anyone. It's not working. CT has not made me able to add fat. I will try coconut oil but I doubt that will change anything. Those of who who are the prime patients for hcg do not do well on fats + hcg. There are some it will work for but it's like everything on the protocol--Dr Simeon tested all kinds of different foods and chose what worked for everyone. he left off things that worked for some and not for others. Fat is one of those things. I wish CT would have changed that. I hoped not to have to give up my cream like I have in other rounds, but I will do it.

I will try CT + hcg and strict protocol after I try coconut and see if that increases losses.

i cant remember where - just go to his blog and search for hcg - but in one of his reports, he wrote that HCG + Coconut OIL do not go together...something about the cold therapy and tropical foods not mixing....seems to me if one was to add fats + hcg they'd do better with Lard? or something to that effect.

are you sure you are not hyper sensitive to butter/cream? i know i'm supposed to avoid all dairy bc of hashi's but sometimes a splash of heavy cream here or there does not outwardly affect me (no bad symptons which i can detect)...however, perhaps it still affects me even in small amounts on some tiny level? maybe it does for you as well and the result is a stall in hcg effectivness? perhaps you'd do better trying lard instead...not sure, just throwing ideas out edited to say : but NOT lard in your coffee, haha

in general i avoid dairy but will have a pat of butter here or there (not right now of course!) and i only splurge on high quality ice cream when it's worth it...for instance, i skipped a scoop of ice cream while at an ice cream parlar a while back bc there is nothing special about their ice cream...however a few weeks ago, someone had some of Jenny's Salted Caramel icecream and you bet i tried it (grassfed milk, high quality natural ingrediants)...and when i make a trip to italy, i'm certainly trying the gelato! i hear the coconut flavor is devine....so ice cream is something i only try about 2-3 times a year. i can live with that

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T01:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17596>

I'm a little bummed. I really hoped it would work. I've been very faithful with CT too. Started the pool at my daughter's back when we talked by phone in early Feb. Then daily swam in 60 degree water for 30 minutes every day for 3 weeks in Hawaii, came home, and went right back to the 50 degree pool every day until we came home in April. Since I got home, I've been doing soaking in a cold tub with ice. I added spot icing when I started the hcg. So I know I've been doing the CT long enough.

But remember, I'm the person hcg was written for. Long term obesity issues. Nothing else worked and I have never been able to vary the protocol. Major fat stores in the hips all my life. Monday I will move to coconut oil and see if it works unless you have a different idea. If still no losses, I'll go back to strict protocol + CT. I love CT! I'm so warm now. I've always been cold, ever since I can remember. i love feeling numb when I get out which is kind of weird.  so I'll be interested in my losses with CT and no fat. Because it doesn't look like fat will work darn it.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T02:38:02+0300

### lisaeh3 — 2012-04-22T03:48:48+0300

### challdo — 2012-04-22T19:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17795>

Hey all

Checking in.

I have just completed the first short round doing CT and keto hCG. Only an occasional nibble of lettuce or a single strawberry during this time. Spread out the meat with a couple of ounces in the a.m. to help hold all the hCG recommended supplements. Felt hungry most days but was strong during my weekly horse riding lessons.

I only lost 12 lbs with 20 shots but really lost inches. My body fat in lbs went down 5.5 lbs which worries me about lean mass lost. This wasnt the case in previous rounds. BMI went from 29.5 to 27.5. Body fat % went from 34.7% to 33.8%. This was not my best round.

I have previously lost 55lbs on this diet with the last 20lbs regained-life and bad diet/schedule. I am also a physician who prescribes this diet, as my observations, Phase 3 is the most critical for control. I did a long round that ended in Jan and went right into Leptin Rx/Leaky gut and stabilized with about a 4 lb weight gain only.

CT felt good and made me wake up. Because of my schedule, I would wake, shower, eat then CT and go to work cold. Had a few detox symptoms but not bad.

I am going to test now. Last testing showed hsCRP around 4-so hoping that is down. Rev T3 ratio was 80 which is good. I am on bioidentical hormones-progesterone and testosterone only. Not menopausal yet....

Cow , pig and lamb are coming soon to my freezer, joined a CSA this weekend. Optimal is in sight...

Cindy

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T19:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17796>

> **nuttmegs17;17837 wrote:** colleen - never asked before - what started the weight gain/obesity issues for you? hashi's? i recall you saying you were quite thin when you were young...i'm just curious about your story

I had a ruptured ovary at 14 and was chubby then. Six months later the other ovary ruptured but the doctor refused to take it out, then the Lord. We babied it along until I had 2 children by 23 and then it was covered with cysts and the size of an orange. The doctor finally removed it along with my uterus. If I knew then what I know now, I would have insisted on being put on thyroid and progesterone but not much was known about hormones back then. The weight gain started then when I went on Premarin.  And it never abated no matter what I did or tried.

Lisa, hcg works even when you are close to your weight goal. Many who want to lose 15 pounds do it their first 2 weeks on the protocol so I don't think it's that I have little to lose. But it was a good guess! That's the way it is with other diets.

### indigogirl — 2012-04-22T22:21:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17867>

So today is my 2nd day at 500 calories without HCG. I stopped the HCG at 42 days and have lost about 26 lbs including the weight I put on for loading (+ 5 Lbs loading).

I ate 500 calories a day no grissini ever, 1/2 grapefruit OR 1/2 an apple for about the first 5 weeks then completely dropped the fruit.

Every morning I had three slices of Gwaltney 40% reduced fat bacon ( 60 calories). For a week I was moving my parents from their house into a independent living complex and I was so active that I increased my protein a bit and occasionally added a whole apple that day. My losses stalled that week...I only lost 1 1/2 lbs that week.

So Im happy with my losses.

Ive bee spot CTing and doing face dunks intermittently for about 2 weeks. I took my first cold bath yesterday and plan to do another one today.

I live in Coastal SC and our tap water is already Luke warm so,I have to add lots of ice. I am going to NY for a week on Wednesday and looking forward to being able to take cold baths!

I probably won't segue into LRx until I return from NY. I am worried about bkfst timing while away.

I will be very conscious of my eating in NY...only meat, fish, chicken and veggies.

I am scared bout adding back fat on the LRx and would love some reassurance from Colleen and those that have segued from HCG to the LRx!

I wonder what it would be like to do it normally as written and just add CT. You know how some courses are just inches and very slow weight loss...I think this just happened. It was an effort to do this a new way but I'm out for a while now. LS is my next goal.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-27T04:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-19225>

It's the extra fat not the CT. If I'm having fat, I'd rather have my coffee the way I like it. LOL And the ones I've talked to who have done it have had my same results with coconut oil. At least most of them. I know ChimpChick did well on her coconut oil but I think she's younger and has a better metabolism than me.

Though CT + hcg without fat doesn't seem to be producing better results either. Some stopped because they got hungry and the others had the same losses. One I talked to today had hoped she wouldn't have to do any steak days and would be more stable than in her other rounds since she teamed it with CT but that hasn't proved to be the case so far in her first week of P3. Her weight has been bouncing around like usual. I should have asked her if she saw different reshaping. I'll do that. I'm hoping for really good reshaping with the cold baths and slow losses but we will see.

Basically I'm beginning to think that hcg doesn't need any help. It affects the hypothalamus all by itself. I actually think I might be losing in this exact same way if I were doing CT +the diet all by itself. I bet the CT would cut the hunger and release the deep fat just like hcg. It seems they might do the same thing and teaming them together does nothing.

But it's all theory. LOL I'll keep trucking along.

### Justfourme — 2012-05-07T15:04:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-21908>

So my typical week one on hcg results in -9.6 lbs. my experiment with week one on hcg plus spot icing, plus some coconut oil netted 3.5 lbs in 1.5 weeks. Was also hungry w cravings and adjusting dose didn't help. After a couple week break, went back to a normal p2 with no icing and no oil, results were -9.3. Hunger was minimal to none, no cravings. So I'd recommend either normal hcg protocol, or else paleo plus CT, but not CT or oils with hcg. Just my N=1 experience.

i for one know something has to be happening..i love to eat, there is no way i'd be able to do this if i was starving. i'm not hungy - just bored sometimes haha - so there is no trickery with this. i also realize this diet is different than taking those pills that just reduce appetitie. i'm hoping robb's sign off on this helps my husband some. he told me last night that he doesnt like that fact that i'm taking hcg...he thinks 500 is too little (and he thought i was doing a short round, not a long one) i tried to explain how it works but i'm not very good at it. i just told him, i'm not hungry, i have energy to still exercise, i think its a good way for me to "Cleanse" my body (since its so strict, its def giving my gut time to heal and should help me pin down any more potential problem foods for me)...plus its working! multiple dr's sign off on this, and i'm doing it responsibly. nothing else i've done has been able to get rid of the "odd fat" i acquired as a result of my hashi's.

ps - colleen - dzguan did not recommend a thyroid increase, and are holding off on the estrogen until next month. they just upped my progesterone a bit and split it into an evening dose towards the end of my cycle. not sure how that will help my fatigue during that time of the month or the extreme dry eye i got around that time, but we'll see!!! my primary doc did suggest i up my cytomel by another 2.5 to see how it goes, so i'll do that. if its too much i'll back off. progesterone in general did help a lot this month - i was not bloated, my boobs were not sore so willing to stick with it!

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-05-12T17:23:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-23388>

I got my response back from SugarFree as follows:

stumbled onto this blog:

[http://myhcgstory.blogspot.com/](http://http://myhcgstory.blogspot.com/)

which has this disconcerting info (this is a letter from sugarfree she got)

"Well, the good news is that your fasting insulin and leptin are just

fine; no worries there. The bad news is that you have pretty severe

thyroid hormone resistance -- in other words, you are, despite your

low TSH (which is actually a pretty useless test since it doesn't

measure anything directly, but by inference), very hypothyroid. Worse:

until you get your ferritin and D levels \*way\* up, there's nothing you

can do about it, since any medication will not be properly absorbed or

used.

As for elevated RT3 in HCG patients, I'm not surprised -- but it's not

the HCG causing that problem; rather a different and even worse double

whammy. Here's what's happening:

1: The HCG turns adipose proto-fat cells into full fat cells, just

waiting to be filled. Not good.

2: The Simeons' protocol -- as written -- drives up fasting insulin,

sometimes into hyperinsulinemea, which drives fat storage over the

long haul, once you're no longer starving yourself. Really bad.

3: The sub-starvation level calories, according to every thyroid

medical textbook ever written, WILL, if done over an extended period

of time (more than 2 or 3 weeks), CAUSE thyroid hormone resistance in

the form of high RT3. The worst.

This is what women are doing to themselves (needlessly, as I've proven

in my blog in so many ways), and this is why I started my blog to

begin with. So that they could see, through Simeons' own words, logic,

and science, and a live experiment -- working so well! -- what HCG

doesn't do (open fat cells or help with weight loss in ANY way), and

what it and the protocol as written does do: trash the metabolism,

make insulin resistance worse, make more permanent fat cells, and

promote thyroid hormone resistance.

Then they wonder why they keep gaining back the weight -- and so

easily. It's a horrible downward spiral, and I am so happy that I have

apparently helped some folks to see the light and give this up

forever."

the one thing i'm seeing from cruising her blog is that she was hungry while on hcg...which tells me she wasn't doing it correctly so she was definitely starving her body and her body was clearly telling her that.

### Claudia — 2012-05-18T03:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-25075>

> **colleencoble;25149 wrote:** Hmm, not finding it, Claudia. Just tons of articles on progesterone but nothing specific that jumps out for application. I read a few of them but don't see anything specific.

Here's the article link: [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/09/18/oral-progesterone-and-progesterone-cream-complications.aspx](http://http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/09/18/oral-progesterone-and-progesterone-cream-complications.aspx)

Progesterone cream must be used cautiously when applied to skin as progesterone is highly fat soluble and will accumulate in fat tissue, thereby disrupting adrenal hormones such as DHEA, cortisol, and testosterone.

Ideally, progesterone cream should NOT be applied to your skin. Instead use mucous epithelial membranes of your labia or rectum. Absorption through these membranes is more complete than through skin, and hormones absorbed through vaginal membranes enter the same pelvic plexus of veins that your ovaries normally empty into.

### differentstory — 2012-05-22T04:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-26172>

> **Claudia;25157 wrote: Here's the article link: [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/09/18/oral-progesterone-and-progesterone-cream-complications.aspx](http://http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/09/18/oral-progesterone-and-progesterone-cream-complications.aspx)
>
>
>
> Progesterone cream must be used cautiously when applied to skin as progesterone is highly fat soluble and will accumulate in fat tissue, thereby disrupting adrenal hormones such as DHEA, cortisol, and testosterone.
>
> Ideally, progesterone cream should NOT be applied to your skin. Instead use mucous epithelial membranes of your labia or rectum. Absorption through these membranes is more complete than through skin, and hormones absorbed through vaginal membranes enter the same pelvic plexus of veins that your ovaries normally empty into.
>
>
> Claudia;25961 wrote:** Did you read the article different story? I was thinking if I need to do this down the road, I would use something like the tube used to apply cream for a yeast infection, like monistat? They sell that over the counter and it includes the applicator. Maybe that would work? Let me know if you feel a difference. According to Mercola, this is more natural to the body and prevents the potential to increase levels too high and have a problem.

I did read the article. I also read the Uzzi (I think?) hormone book (cant remember; it's been awhile) and he mentioned that you need a different "make up" for different applications. I thought I had read that gels were what should be applied to the areas "down there" (said in my best 50 shades voice).

But I was just going to try it once and see what happened. That's a good idea - those applicators. I was going to wait until my current syringe was almost empty and just use that to apply the last dose, but I bet it won't be long enough.

I will say that this morning I applied the cream to my upper arms only per Colleen's post. Normally, I will just slather it on my whole inside arm (upper and lower). Then I got dressed, cooked breakfast, ate, and then I got very nauseous for over an hour, vomited, and went back to bed. Ugh. What a way to start the week! My gut reaction was that the different application definitely allowed the cream to have more of an effect on me. I will try again tomorrow and see if the same thing happens.

### Claudia — 2012-05-23T04:35:03+0300

I know 2 women on my Hashi's group on Facebook, and of course that isn't a lot, but they both gained a lot of weight from using progesterone cream. One of them is using an Apex supplement that has worked great for her estrogen dominance, much better than the progesterone cream.

I also have a friend who was on progesterone cream and felt sick and discontinued. I don't think it was vomiting or nausea.

Sorry you felt sick and hope it didn't happen again.

I know someone else, but she was in menopause, who loves progesterone cream and slathers it on all the time. She says she feels fabulous.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-09T13:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-57074>

Hi Colleen,

I'm starting day three of HCG tomorrow after starting the paleo diet a month ago (and finding Jack's website just before that), I thought I would give HCG a go... but having read as much as I can here - and its not all of it as I have to go to bed now (its late), I wondered could you please tell me - do I stick to the HCG protocol and not substitute grain for more protein? And do I add in CT or not? Thank you

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-10T00:42:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-57186>

> **Amber Tyrrell;58376 wrote:** Hi Colleen,
>
> I'm starting day three of HCG tomorrow after starting the paleo diet a month ago (and finding Jack's website just before that), I thought I would give HCG a go... but having read as much as I can here - and its not all of it as I have to go to bed now (its late), I wondered could you please tell me - do I stick to the HCG protocol and not substitute grain for more protein? And do I add in CT or not? Thank you

What I've seen is:

1\. CT seems to interfere with some of the HCG magic (loss of hunger). I had a very good first round, and dropped CT. I think if you wanted, I would get into your HCG groove FIRST so you could know if the CT were messing up your hunger signals. Jack will say I'm nuts that CT can only help, but that's not been some N-1 experiences. If it were me, and you are doing something as damaging as only eating 500 calories a day for 4-6 weeks, I'd want to have it be as successful as possible and learn from successes that have traveled the road before you.

Jack is a man, his N-1 is simply different in a major way.

2 - I'm assuming you mean substitute protein for grain? Meaning NO TOAST, NO GRAIN. . .yes. Drop the grain. Don't eat grains. Up your calories to the 500 with more protein.

I know I'm not Colleen - but I though I would throw in a different $.02. Good Luck. Follow the protocol even if it does seem silly.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-10T18:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-57340>

> **Amber Tyrrell;58575 wrote:** Thank you Cavemam... you have given great advice and I will follow it. I just finished my first day of the 500 cal diet today, and I am so out of eating grains and fruit that I never even thought of them, so didn't have the grossini or fruit but instead I doubled my protein at both meals. I must admit I felt hungry this afternoon - will that start to ease? Already thinking about those BIG ASSED BREAKFASTS!! I really hope this works because its difficult to do. It's murphy's law, but we really don't go out much at night and in the last week I have been invited to 4 partys within the time I'm doing HCG! Damn!

You can do it!!! I had my 4th of July weeklong family vacation and packed all of my food, so damnit, you can't handle a party! (channeling Jack).

Dosing/hunger is the hardest thing to dial in! What HCG are you taking, how much? give details. First 4 days are the hardest - so many things to adjust to. The good part is you have the big weight losses so that motivates.

Colleen is the dosing master, but chances are if you are feeling major hunger/cravings you are taking too much. I can only speak to the subq injections.

Two types of hunger to be aware of. . .

1\. actual physical discomfort in the tummy area, like it's moving around going "hey bitch, what's up with this!"

2\. cravings, wanting to eat!

Lastly, I think you may want to consider adding in either strawberries OR an apple for late meal each day. There are some extra properties these fruits have that assist with all kinds of biological functions - fructose/carbs are NOT the only consideration when it comes to fruit. Your body is going through ALOT- help it how you can and apples have something that help you keep fat burning. If you you are low cal/in ketosis. I did not take supplements, but in retrospect would do it if I did it again. Search Jack's blog - he lays out a list of them.

### Charlotte — 2012-11-18T19:16:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65168>

Hi everybody, it took me almost all sunday to read this thread with little chores inbetween. Like filling up my new whirlpool with water. It came out a perfect 50Â°F. I am so glad the loading days are over as I hate to eat more than I need. But I ate mostly fat, no carbs which I haven't eaten anyway since 3 months. Since then, I have been in ketosis. I am keen on trying the hCG in connection with CT. I need to find out myself, how or if that works. I still think it might for me. If not I stop and put the temperature up and enjoy the snow from my hot tub. This time, I took my measurements to see the loss in cm also. I had no problems going from hCG to Epi-Paleo, even lost another 2 kg, feeling good physically and mentally. Diabetes is history since then. The only changes I will do from last time is lowering the dose (took 1500 i.U. first injection before noticing my error and went down then to 150 i.U. ), having breakfast and another meal later in the day and trying CT. It is so good for the skin. Will see how it goes. Strict Dr. S. protocol, no grain, little fruit or none. I will keep you posted.

### LinD — 2012-11-18T19:31:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65169>

> **colleencoble;66571 wrote:** You should stop after about 47 days. Personally, I would not do CT until after P3.

I've seen that same conclusion. CT not working with hCG protocol.

### Charlotte — 2012-11-19T08:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65285>

First morning after loading same weight as when started loading. 1st day 300 g gain, 2nd day 300 g lost. CT in the evening. Took forever to get warm, but deep sleep and very warm in the morning. And the liver area did hurt after CT.

### colleencoble — 2012-11-19T17:31:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65355>

Charlotte, if you're losing on eli-paleo, why bother with hcg?

### Charlotte — 2012-11-27T17:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65717>

Colleen, because I do not want to waste any more time. I still have a lot of weight to lose and I want to get it done. I have never lost so much weight with so much ease in such a short time than with hCG and even though I miss eating Epi-Paleo I know that I will come back to it the minute I am off hCG. And believe me, just thinking about Epi-Paleo gets me so creative in terms of thinking up recipes. I even figured out a recipe for a German Chocolate cake, fully Epi-Paleo which is a deviation from my grandmas grand German Chocolate cake, and it will taste just as outstanding. Or think about a white almond torte or a Linzer Torte and all fully Epi-Paleo. I think that hCG is great education in terms of eating.

My first week is over now and I am happy with the result. I lost 3 kg which is 6,6 pounds the first week which is more than in my first round of hCG. I do CT (not every day, because it is sometimes difficult due to business matters) but on the weekends and at least 1-2 times during the week. I feel much warmer even though it takes me a few hours to get warm. I speed through the house afterwards doing all kinds of household chores (which is funny because I am not really a housewife) to get warm and it is so invigorating. The first CT I did in the evening and that was a mistake. I could not sleep and when I finally did, my sleep was deep, but too short because I had a normal working day the next day. So after that I now do it first thing in the morning and that works really fine. For me, it works. So I think that everybody needs to find out for themselves what works and what does not. It definitely speeds up my metabolism and I feel very strong and flexible afterwards. I do it for 30 minutes and the water is a little colder now than 50Â°F, but it is quite ok for me. I get out when my feet get really cold and it becomes very uncomfortable. My neighbour thinks I am crazy as he watches me from his window on my back porch. And as I am lying there I am watching the sparrows and the crows in my cherry tree and it is so peaceful.

### colleencoble — 2012-11-27T17:35:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65718>

So happy it's working for you! Just monitor how you feel and see how it goes. Lots of things work the first week and then don't but maybe you'll be fortunate enough that CT with hcg continues to work. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Charlotte — 2012-12-02T15:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-66157>

almost two weeks now into VLCD. Total loss this round so far 4 kg which is app. 9 pounds. CT 2-3 times per week half an hr. in lower temperature than 50Â°F. (Its snowing in Germany and temperatures are below freezing, so pool is VERY cold)Â  No complaints so far. Not hungry at all. Could fast all day, but don't because the only thing that worries me is that I do not get enough nutrients. So now I am thinking that I will not do another round after this but stick to Epi-Paleo although the weight loss is slower, but getting slim is not everything, getting healthy is much more important.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-07T12:09:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-69742>

ok, as promised here my results. I did 46 days on hCG with a break every sunday from the injection. Lost 27 pounds, which is real close to the same as first round. Did CT (not every day and sometimes could not take 30 minutes as I was just too cold) but all in all CT gave me at least not a lesser result, but not a better either.
I am now close to my normal weight (another 17,6 pounds) . I now have to adjust to eating normal again which for me now is upping my fat content, which proofs a little difficult as I am not used to the fat anymore and my gut and liver must be adapted slowly as otherwise I get pains.
Labs improved very much, especially leptin, BG, alpha-amylase, lipids, LDL, HDL, food intolerances, etc.
Total weight loss so far since 27th of August 2012: 59,9 pounds.
Next round of hCG scheduled for beginning of March to get somewhere between normal and ideal weight, then I will see. Since I never had normal weight I do not know where my personal feel-good zone will be. In the meantime it is maintanance and some more pounds loss, like between the last two rounds. I plan on doing the high fat diet with 1000 calories of which 90% is fat and 10% is protein as soon as I am used to fat again and now upping CT to every day.
Also now I will do some strength training to increase muscle tone and lose my "chicken wings".

### caroline — 2013-01-07T16:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-69769>

great success story Charlotte! I am interested to know what you are eating now? You say ...90% fat and 10% protein? What does a day of food look like for you now?

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T02:29:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-69819>

Hi Caroline, I call it a success, when I am done and able to keep the weight off. Until then I am only on the way to success.
1000 calories is not a lot especially not when 90% is fat. So 100 calories come from protein which means, app. 125 g of salmon for 25 g of protein (x 4 = 100 kcal) and 100 g come from Fat (x 9 = 900 kcal). So with 125 g of salmon I have the protein for the day and 13 g of fat. That leaves 87 g of fat which I get from app. 3 avocados or other fat. Or I do quail eggs raw in a protein shake with oil.
I just look for the best foods, look up their protein, fat and carb content and calculate from there. This I want to do for 2 weeks and see. Then I decide my next move,.
Its all trial and error or success.
Coming off hCG you have to be very careful. Especially fats need to be added slowly at least with me so I do not burden my liver.

# Thread: CT weight loss results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T00:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-2974>

I've been CT'ing for a bit over a week now. I'm just getting into the baths but at a wimpy 68 deg. I haven't had any help in the fat loss department yet. I've been doing face dunks, cool showers and tons of spot icing on stomach, whole back, legs.

How long til others noticed fat loss and was it only after being able to do full on ice baths?

Thanks!

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T01:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-2999>

I'm a guy, so we're different (you probably knew that). I started CT'ing in bitter cold Alaskan air and doing all I could to stay cooler than normal. I had been on a stall since November, but after about 3 weeks, I was down 5 lbs. I'm pretty much at my goal weight, but have that spare tire, loose skin we all get--thyat seems to be disappearing very well.

I hope you get serious and give this a good try, there's not many people doing this and everybody we have doing it and reporting back will let us know how it works. It's definitely not a 'get skinny overnight' kinda deal.

Good luck!

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T01:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3008>

I won't be giving up, that is for sure. I'm in this for way more than weight loss, but I'm certainly looking forward to that. I'm only looking to lose about 7 pounds, so hopefully it will happen fast once it starts.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-20T02:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3043>

5 lbs. down since starting CT in mid-Feb.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T03:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3077>

No weight lost since starting 4 weeks ago, but BF-percentage went down quite fast in the first week, and muscle is really coming out... so i think I've lsot fat but gained muscle (I lift heavy).

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-20T07:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3162>

I'm down nearly 10 pounds since starting CT February 22nd.

### [email redacted] — 2012-03-20T10:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3174>

A week in and I've gained 5lbs but lost 2 inches around the tummy. Baths are daily and getting colder after my first "too cold" one, and my daily shower is now cold as well! Even better in some ways is that our main source of heat for the house is no longer safe to use, so the top temperature inside is now a balmy 53.6F, and I'm resisting adding layers

### David — 2012-03-20T13:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3186>

Two weeks of face dunks (57F skin temp), a week of concurrent 30 minute cold showers (63-64F water temp) in the sixth week of Leptin Rx: daily BAB, 6 hours between meals, no snacks, nothing after seven-thirty PM, less than 25 g carbs daily, all Rx supplements recommended by Dr. Kruse and I sleep in a pitch black bedroom. Since beginning CT my weight is up two pounds. I had been following a Paleo diet since last November and lost 35 pounds. I will start the next step, icing the torso today.

### MartiD — 2012-03-20T14:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3188>

Being doing baths pretty consistent since end of February and added ice spotting belly this week. Haven't done measurements but am down about 3-4 pounds plus reshaping hips and waste. Working on lower belly and thighs still but I also have the funny feeling fat....seems softer, lighter if that makes sense!

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-20T14:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-weight-loss-results.305/post-3200>

down 3 lbs in one week since starting the ice packs.

Some background - I have been at this current plateau for many many years after losing 50 llbs on a low carb/paleo type diet.

I haven't changed my eating or exercising pattern - the only thing different is the CT.

# Thread: Stretch marks clearing up anyone?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T00:37:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-2978>

On the monster thread at MDA somewhere Jack mentioned stretch marks healing with his keto diet and oral and topical application of coconut oil, vitamin K and a few other things I don't remember (and can't seem to find in that thread again, big surprise).

Has this happened for anyone here yet? There are a million things that will take the color out of stretch marks, but what I want to hear about it the actual scar tissue going away- so it actually looks like new skin again.

I've been eating clean, applying coconut oil, drinking bone broth and eating lots of things high in K2. I don't ingest a ton of coconut oil because I don't really feel very good on it. I'm not sure that I don't have a sensitivity there actually. Anyway, if this has been working for anyone I would LOVE to hear all about it.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-20T02:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3035>

Reseveratrol and tumeric with caprylic acid.....

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T02:43:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3036>

I've done everything Jack suggests minus the reservatrol. The stretchies from my last pregnancy 17 months ago are all gone. The bigger, deeper ones I got after the twins 5 years ago are still there, but fading. I actually think they'll be gone within 3-4 years.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T02:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3048>

> **Jack Kruse;3057 wrote:** Reseveratrol and tumeric with caprylic acid.....

Are those for both oral and topical application? Thanks a TON in advance...

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T02:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3050>

> **Glamorama;3058 wrote:** I've done everything Jack suggests minus the reservatrol. The stretchies from my last pregnancy 17 months ago are all gone. The bigger, deeper ones I got after the twins 5 years ago are still there, but fading. I actually think they'll be gone within 3-4 years.

Is your scar tissue lessening or do you mean the color? Mine are invisible in color, but I'm looking for the scar tissue to go away. It will always look wrinkly I think until the skin has truly healed back into regular tissue.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T03:31:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3075>

> **PaleoMom;3072 wrote:** Is your scar tissue lessening or do you mean the color? Mine are invisible in color, but I'm looking for the scar tissue to go away. It will always look wrinkly I think until the skin has truly healed back into regular tissue.

Its gone, I can't see my newest stretchmarks anymore. Not even in day light!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-20T03:32:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3076>

> **PaleoMom;3070 wrote:** Are those for both oral and topical application? Thanks a TON in advance...

I'd think he means oral. The coconut oil (caprylic acid) you can also use on your skin, but its what you feed yourself thatw ill determine the healing of your skin (mostly).

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T04:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-3094>

You're probably right. Thanks for sharing your experience too!

### MJ* — 2012-03-24T03:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5285>

> **Jack Kruse;3057 wrote:** Reseveratrol and tumeric with caprylic acid.....

How much of each of these do you need to take and how often? 1x a day? Also, is Red Wine Extract with Resveratrol from Sundown ok for Resveratrol or is there a better product?

### MJ* — 2012-03-24T04:00:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5286>

> **Glamorama;3097 wrote:** Its gone, I can't see my newest stretchmarks anymore. Not even in day light!

That's fantastic...I wold love to have that happen! My first born was a big baby and watching those marks spread was so depressing...

### chewingthefat — 2012-03-24T04:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5299>

I think my few, small varicose veins have pretty much gone away. The color of my stretch marks seems to have improved, but the extensive scars are still there. I am doing a slower CT than is suggested...will see as it progresses. I tend to cycle 2 weeks co-q 10 ubiquinol, then 2 weeks reservatrol, but will invest in full time reservatrol and see if it helps. Will add the tumeric and already eat lots of coconut oil as well as using it as my only moisturizer. I leave it on as I brush my teeth, fix my hair and then use a towel to lightly scrub it off before putting on clothes. Great for exfoliation! My skin look fabulous. Love to hear other's progress in this area as well.

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-24T06:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5335>

Ahhh I hope my stretch marks go away! I even started getting the marks before I became very overweight in my early 20s!  I haven't noticed any improvement in them but I'm gonna keep my fingers crossed.

### Inger — 2012-03-24T07:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5340>

Reading this, I had to take a look on my own stretchmarks that I have had on my inner, upper legs for many years.

They are very minor, no pregnancies, I guess they are from times me beeing 20 pounds overweight 15 years ago. I never was fat. But these strechmarks I got anyway. Okay - they are almost gone!!!! Or, lets say, on the left leg there are NOTHING anymore, on the right only very very little I cannot see them if I dont stretch my skin a little actually. How cool is that!

I have this varicous veins very minor too but I feel they are less visible too. I am sure it is so. This is only from cold showers, no baths yet. 3 weeks into CT.

### MartiD — 2012-03-24T15:42:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5391>

Mine definitely looks better plus I have a two year old scar on belly from a spinal fusion that will never disappear but looks a lot better. Mine has made the most improvement in last month with CT! All my skin is better!

### tweety — 2012-03-24T21:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stretch-marks-clearing-up-anyone.306/post-5542>

I am waiting for mine. I have very stretched out skin on my belly from my pregnancies, and as of yet they are the same. But I check them daily just in case! If I could stand the thought of hard evidence I would take a photo in case they do improve later. But I am not sure I want that floating out there!

If the healing and retracing theory is correct, then I would think the newer marks would heal sooner, and the longer ago you got the stretch marks the longer it will take to heal.

# Thread: Is it poseible to go from LR to LS in less than 2 weeks?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T00:37:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-2979>

A little background ... I was LC Primal for nearly two years before just recently simultaneously doing the LRx and CT. Before starting, I was stalled at about 15-20 pounds of fat from ideal body composition. I was in good health, but not optimal at all. Since, I've lost at least 5-6 pounds of fat and have all the signs of LS in his Leptin FAQ, but didn't really have these signs before starting the LRx & CT (which makes me think I was LR before).

But is that possible? Dr. K says 6-8 weeks, but also said CT speeds it up. But that fast?

What do you all think? Possible?

PS - Sorry about the typo in the title. If a mod sees this, please correct. Thanks.

### L8F — 2012-03-20T00:39:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-2981>

I would say that it is, esp since you were already LC Primal for so long giving you a nice jumpstart to all the bad stuff--meaning grains and leaky-ness. Also you have new signs, and didn't have them before. I think for me it took just over 4 weeks, and possibly sooner...

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-20T11:08:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3175>

> **L8F;3003 wrote:** I would say that it hhis, esp since you were already LC Primal for so long giving you a nice jumpstart to all the bad stuff--meaning grains and leaky-ness. Also you have new signs, and didn't have them before. I think for me it took just over 4 weeks, and possibly sooner...

Thanks. I'm going to give it a couple more weeks, just in case, then I'll move to the Post Leptin Reset protocol with CT for a bit longer to see if I can get to improved/ideal body comp before the summer. Then, I hope to enjoy a short summer before going keto and doing CT, starting in late summer. I guess the bottom line is I feel much better than before I started, and I'm filled with optimism.

Once I fully fix myself, I'm looking forward to use this info to help others. So freakin exciting!

### mattlovell — 2012-03-20T21:08:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3434>

hi - where do you find the leptin diet please?

thanks!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T21:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3435>

Mattlovell - google search "My leptin prescription"; I think that's the title of D.r Kruse's main LR post.

evo mama - what is the CT protocol you've been using (type, frequency, duration...)?

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-21T01:51:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-poseible-to-go-from-lr-to-ls-in-less-than-2-weeks.307/post-3636>

> **MamaGrok;3460 wrote:** Mattlovell - google search "My leptin prescription"; I think that's the title of D.r Kruse's main LR post.
>
>
>
> evo mama - what is the CT protocol you've been using (type, frequency, duration...)?

Wake up, quick progressively cold shower (10 min), BAB, facial plunges (10 min), all of that daily, then 30-45 min baths every other night. Water out of tap is about 50 degrees. No real icing yet.

Yes, I too want to be the 80+ yr old with the six pack like Dr. K explains of in his practice.  I feel confident that it's possible too w/ eating to circadian cycles, seasonal and using CT. Ya!

### Guest — 2012-04-12T15:49:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-14201>

I was just thinking this morning...we don't see many obese 80 year olds, let alone 100. The two 100 year olds that I know personally are both thin and have been all their lives.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-12T16:02:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-14205>

My 80yo grandfather is obese, but he's also Type 2 diabetic, wheelchair-ridden, and likely has less than 5 years to live. So so so sad.

He was fit and athletic his whole life until medications left him increasingly unable to use his legs. At that point, he was at the mercy of the SAD fare fed to him by others and just went downhill super fast after that.

You are completely right - I see 90yo's in WAP's work with few wrinkles (who have spent their whole lives in the South Pacific sun), 62yo's hauling loads up Alpen slopes with no problem, elsewhere have seen 100yo's with beautiful clear singing voices (so much for the "age" argument to so many divas' decline after 50), my 92yo other grandfather riding a bike & taking up cooking again (after a 70yo hiatus) after his wife died, and much more. _A long decline is not inevitable_. I love the stories of the 90+ women keeling over while mowing the lawn ... no twenty year decline into decrepitude.

My grandfather died at 94, living independently until 3 months earlier, including continuing his tool sharpening business. My great-grandmother on the other side died at 94, living independently until a year or two earlier. I remember she was still a waitress in her late 80's. My husband's grandmother died in her late 80s, living independently until shortly before. They were all fairly to very healthy just one year before death.

OTOH, our parents are beginning the decline in their late 50s and 60s into deafness, loss of mobility, bone breakage, and so much more.

My sisters and their husbands are beginning the decline into surgery and cancer and organ removal in their 30s. It breaks my heart. Pottenger's cats, people.

### Guest — 2012-04-12T17:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-14228>

@Momagrok---I will never ever forgive the most allopathic doctors and pharmsecuetical scientists because of the havoc they have caused for those who put faith in them. I did for a short time but NEVER again.

### Glamorama — 2012-04-13T07:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-14614>

Imagine our kids. No wonder my entire mothers group and our kids have food allergies... just one generation ago it was rare, now they all suffer form one or the other. Its catching up with us. Pottenger's cats indeed.

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-29T21:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-19901>

I've noticed a lot of my friend'sand acquaintances from high school whom I keep in touch with on Facebook (we're all in our mid twenties) are having trouble getting pregnant. Like, an inordinate amount of them are resorting to fertility treatments, seems like

More than half of us have had c-sections, and lots of preemies and so-called "miracle babies." This should not be happening to twenty-five -28 year olds. Pottenger knew it all along.

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-29T22:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-19914>

I read about cows who were fed GMO grains having a lot lower fertility rate. If your generation could avoid all GMO food and anything grown using Roundup, these numbers might still change.

### Glamorama — 2012-04-30T07:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-20021>

> **BeingVenus;19974 wrote:** I've noticed a lot of my friend'sand acquaintances from high school whom I keep in touch with on Facebook (we're all in our mid twenties) are having trouble getting pregnant. Like, an inordinate amount of them are resorting to fertility treatments, seems like
>
> More than half of us have had c-sections, and lots of preemies and so-called "miracle babies." This should not be happening to twenty-five -28 year olds. Pottenger knew it all along.

Wow now that you mention it, its true! In fact, I'm baffled at the amount of skinny, healthy-looking mums I see whos truggle to conceive. I've known a lot of obese woman who had a hard time, and in a way that made sense... but even the leaner young woman struggle. I'm tipping the low-fat hysteria is partly to blame.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-30T18:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-20093>

Processed food, processed food, processed food. Then low-fat, low-fat, low-fat = no fat-soluble nutrients to build a healthy baby. High stress with little satisfying ingredients to life (mostly entertainment-driven) = pregnenolone steal = not enough progesterone. Sy ormula, soy in everything, BCPs early on = hormonal chaos. Cannot conceive this way.

I think the infertility epidemic (yet to come) may be our only hope in waking up. People can think their parents & grandparents were "just as unhealthy," "struggled with weight after 30yo," blah blah blah, but they KNOW they had babies. We all "know" miscarriages are 1 in 4; when infertility and miscarriages become 1 in 2, society \*might\* just wake up.

They'll probably blame it on solar flares & farting cows first.

### MJ* — 2012-04-30T20:07:56+0300

### Rodney — 2012-10-06T06:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-56572>

Hi Dextery,

Never heard about Ex Mr. Universe Manohar Aich before so thanks for sharing this informative link with others. I will be kind if you will share more pictures or workout videos of Pocket Hercules with us please! Thanks in advance for any input.

### Marccus — 2012-10-27T08:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-60415>

unbelievable! what is the secret of the health of this angry old man?? can we live this much long life i dont think so it is possible

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-27T15:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-60425>

Here is the secret of his health: Write down what you have learned. I use my blog as my journal. If you write down what you have learned, it’s helps you gathering up all of the "bits" of knowledge and experience you’ve collected in life to help you explore the things you don’t know and where never aware of. It is like seeing life constantly as a child does. It is a pretty amazing way to live anew.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-01T17:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/indian-100-year-old-strong-man-what-is-your-excuse.308/post-61288>

> **piano-doctor-lady;19987 wrote:** I read about cows who were fed GMO grains having a lot lower fertility rate. If your generation could avoid all GMO food and anything grown using Roundup, these numbers might still change.

[http://farmandranchfreedom.org/gmo-miscarriages](http://http://farmandranchfreedom.org/gmo-miscarriages)

The roundup ready grains have a "bug" (some discussion yet as to the nature of said bug) that does increase miscarriages and infertility.

Due to the nature of genetic drift, it's not going away.if you raise heirloom corn, and ADM or someone has a field of RR dent corn down the road, you'll get a bit of cross pollination. Not all will take, but eventually, some will.

I grew up on a wheat farm, and we received monsantos publications for years. (Reality is that most farmers don't like chemical usage, but truly can not afford to not. With land payments, diesel prices, and implement costs, anyone who thinks otherwise is terrifically uninformed) dad and I had many discussions about RR stuff when it was first heralded as the savior.

I regret to say that we foresaw problems...not of this variety, of course, but in general. As things continue to cross pollinate, the genetics will continue to spread. If you must eat grains, sticking with diploidal varieties (eg einkorn) is, IMO, the safest route. Doubt that any one here WILL ever eat wheat again, but just in case.

Wow. Epic derail. Sorry guys!

Unfortunately, one cannot unstrike a match.

[http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/Item01568/Mitochondrial-Energy-Optimizer-with-BioPQQ.html?source=search&key=PQQ](http://http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/Item01568/Mitochondrial-Energy-Optimizer-with-BioPQQ.html?source=search&key=PQQ)

Unfortunately, I live in Canada and some of the ingredients are restricted for shipment. I have found a brand in Canada which has 20mg PQQ and 300mg co-enzyme CoQ10 (unfortunately not the ubiquinol type). Here is the link:

[http://www.aor.ca/html/products.php?id=256](http://http://www.aor.ca/html/products.php?id=256)

I just noticed you are from Australia. I hope those leads are useful were you live. Good luck in your search.

# Thread: CT during warm weather
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoDentist — 2012-03-20T02:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3045>

Dr. Kruse is all about staying within the rhythms of nature. light/dark, seasons, eating seasonally, etc...

Is anyone concerned that doing CT in the summer or in warm climates is somehow against the natural order of things.

what I mean it, doing CT then walking out into the hot outside...won't this screw something up????

I'd love to hear what Dr. Kruse thinks!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-20T02:54:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3047>

I do most of my CT in the Summer by swimming.....and Icing on my deck

### colleencoble — 2012-03-20T02:56:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3049>

So normal water temp in the summer is helpful? then you just add ice?

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-20T02:57:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3052>

For as long as I have disease to cure, or weight to lose, I will stay in ketogenic paleo winter. The rythms of nature are for the healthy to enjoy, and once I am, I will.

What is against the natural order of things is the way I lived for 52 years. I need something radical to reverse that.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-20T03:02:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3058>

Every location is different.. but- where I am the nights dip to the low 40's all summer long, even when we have 90+ deg days. Also, our local lake is quite cool for swimming all summer long. Historically, humans would have had to remain adapted to the cold during summer to stay warm during the cold nights and they would have been bathing in the cold water all summer long too.

### AKMan — 2012-03-20T03:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3078>

I was on the beach near Tampa yesterday. I got a kick out of watching little kids get out of the water and shiver like crazy...their teeth were chattering so hard you'da thought it was Alaska!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-20T03:57:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-during-warm-weather.310/post-3089>

> **AKMan;3100 wrote:** I was on the beach near Tampa yesterday. I got a kick out of watching little kids get out of the water and shiver like crazy...their teeth were chattering so hard you'da thought it was Alaska!

That's sad. I grew up going to the beach near Tampa in spring, summer, and occasionally winter. It's not even that cold in the Gulf in December. March, and especially after the crazy mild winter we've had in the south, should be great. Sad wussy kids these days.

My dd used to freeze getting in the water in the summer at our pool when she was 6. Our son is now that age (and even less body fat than she had) and she is 13, and neither of them freeze. WAY different O6 scenario than we had 7 years ago in our house.

just bumping this thread in the hoped Dr Kruse chimes in.

and aren't mismatches between the natural environment and the artificial one humans create the basis of mismatched and disease????

here's how I see this - yes, you are creating a mismatch,with the seasons/circadian cycles. However leveraging cold and the ancient pathway to reverse disease (remember the ancient pathway bypasses the circadian cycles, via the leptin melancortal pathway - so it no longer becomes a mismatch) . So if you have disease - say inflammation, diabetes etc - and you follow Paleo WOE, and eat seasonally and stick to the circadian rhythms - all you do is slow down the further progression of the disease. by CTing, and eating Keto you Stop the disease, and begin to undo the harm, which will bring you back into balance, and allow you to follow Paleo woe in harmony w/seasons and circadian rhythms. Depending on your issues it can take from 18-24mths to become cold adapted, and reverse the disease.

Dr K will correct me if I'm out of bounds...

Think about the origins of mammals, especially those that are cold adapted - think whales - they leverage cold adaption; and don't rely on light cycles - the branch of mammals we originated from - came from the deep sea...its only been recent that we've become warm adapted and out of synch w/nature

### MartiD — 2012-04-09T17:20:00+0300
